description:

Current data shows:
- 'orch' topic: 29 investigations (!)
- Other topics with 3+ investigations each

- synthesis: topics needing consolidation (3+ investigations)
- promote: kn entries ready for kb promotion
- stale: decisions with 0 citations, >7 days old
- timestamp: when suggestions were generated

UI usage:
- 'Knowledge Debt' panel showing topics needing synthesis
- Badge count of actionable items
- Links to specific investigations

This surfaces 'what knowledge work is piling up' - helps prioritize documentation work.

title: Add kb reflect --type open to surface investigations with unimplemented recommendations
description:
kb reflect currently finds clusters by topic prefix but misses open action items.

**Problem:** Investigations with actionable 'Next:' recommendations get buried. Example: FAILURE_REPORT.md template recommended in inv-failure-mode-artifacts but never implemented.

**Solution:** Add --type open mode that:
1. Finds investigations where Status ≠ Complete  
2. Extracts the 'Next:' field
3. Filters out close/none/complete/implemented
4. Shows what's actionable

**Why not LLM:** D.E.K.N. structure already standardizes where recommendations live. Pattern matching on Next: field is fast, free, and deterministic. Template is the forcing function.

**Test:** rg '\*\*Next:\*\*' .kb/investigations/*.md | grep -vi 'close|none|complete|implemented'
Currently returns 18 open items that kb reflect doesn't surface.

title: Add outputs.required to artifact-producing skills
description:
## Problem

Only `investigation` skill has `outputs.required` defined. Other artifact-producing skills have no verification gates.

## Skills to Update

| Skill | Artifact Pattern | Structure Checks |
|-------|------------------|------------------|
| systematic-debugging | `.kb/investigations/{date}-*debug*.md` | D.E.K.N., self-review |
| reliability-testing | `.kb/investigations/{date}-*reliability*.md` | D.E.K.N., go/no-go section |
| research | `.kb/investigations/{date}-*research*.md` | D.E.K.N., recommendation section |
| architect | `.kb/decisions/{date}-*.md` OR `.kb/investigations/{date}-*.md` | D.E.K.N. |
| codebase-audit | `.kb/investigations/{date}-*audit*.md` | D.E.K.N., findings section |

## Implementation

Add to each skill's `.skillc/skill.yaml`:

```yaml
outputs:
  required:
    - pattern: ".kb/investigations/{date}-*[pattern]*.md"
      description: "[Skill] file with findings"
  optional:
    - pattern: ".kb/decisions/{date}-*.md"
      description: "Promoted decision (if recommendation accepted)"
```

## Not Covered (different verification needed)

- **feature-impl** - Output is commits, not files
- **issue-creation** - Output is beads issue
- **design-session** - Output varies (epic/investigation/decision)
- **kb-reflect** - Output is kb actions

These need verification in `orch complete`, not `skillc verify`.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] systematic-debugging has outputs.required
- [ ] reliability-testing has outputs.required  
- [ ] research has outputs.required
- [ ] architect has outputs.required
- [ ] codebase-audit has outputs.required
- [ ] All skills rebuild with `skillc build`
- [ ] `skillc verify` works on each skill
close_reason: Added outputs.required to 4 artifact-producing skills (codebase-audit, architect, research, reliability-testing). All verify tests pass. Committed to ~/.claude/skills and orch-go.

title: Add OpenCode launchd plist for auto-start and KeepAlive
description:
OpenCode serve is the foundation that orch daemon and orch serve depend on, but it has no auto-start mechanism.

Current state:
- com.orch.daemon.plist exists (orch daemon run)
- com.orch-go.serve.plist exists (orch serve)  
- NO opencode launchd plist - manual start required

Problem: When OpenCode crashes or returns errors (like 'redirected too many times'), the entire orchestration system stalls. Daemon can't spawn, orch serve can't query sessions.

Deliverable:
1. Create ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.opencode.serve.plist
   - ProgramArguments: opencode serve --port 4096
   - RunAtLoad: true
   - KeepAlive: true (restart on crash)
   - StandardOutPath: ~/.orch/logs/opencode.log
   - StandardErrorPath: ~/.orch/logs/opencode.err
   - EnvironmentVariables: PATH with node/bun locations

2. Test: launchctl bootstrap, verify auto-restart on kill

3. Document in orchestrator skill under 'System Maintenance'

Reference: ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.orch.daemon.plist for pattern
close_reason: Implemented directly: Created ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.opencode.serve.plist with RunAtLoad and KeepAlive. Verified auto-restart works.

This connects to the System Learning Loop - we track context gaps, we should also track fix reliability gaps.

title: [orch-go] investigation: test third concurrent spawn
Consolidate session-context.js and orch-session-autostart.ts into single plugin
description:
## Problem

Two separate OpenCode plugins with overlapping concerns:

| Plugin | Purpose | Event |
|--------|---------|-------|
| session-context.js | Loads orchestrator skill | config hook |
| orch-session-autostart.ts | Runs orch session start | session.created |

Both:
- Detect workers (same logic, duplicated)
- Run at session start
- Are orchestrator-specific
- Live in ~/.config/opencode/plugin/

## Why Consolidate

1. **DRY** - isWorker() logic duplicated across both files
2. **Consistency** - Same detection should use same code
3. **Maintenance** - Single point of change for orchestrator session setup
4. **Clarity** - One 'orchestrator session plugin' vs two partial plugins

## Proposed Solution

Create single `orchestrator-session.ts` plugin that:
1. Uses `config` hook to inject orchestrator skill (from session-context.js)
2. Uses `session.created` to auto-start orch session (from orch-session-autostart.ts)
3. Shares isWorker() detection logic
4. Removes the two old plugins

## Implementation

```typescript
export const OrchestratorSessionPlugin: Plugin = async ({ directory, $ }) => {
  // Shared worker detection
  const worker = await isWorker(directory)
  
  return {
    config: async (config) => {
      if (worker) return
      // Inject orchestrator skill
      config.instructions.push(skillPath)
    },
    event: async ({ event }) => {
      if (event.type !== 'session.created') return
      if (worker) return
      // Auto-start orch session
      await $\`orch session start\`
    }
  }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Single orchestrator-session.ts plugin created
- [ ] session-context.js removed
- [ ] orch-session-autostart.ts removed
- [ ] Both behaviors preserved (skill injection + session auto-start)
- [ ] Worker detection shared, not duplicated

## References

- ~/.config/opencode/plugin/session-context.js
- ~/.config/opencode/plugin/orch-session-autostart.ts
- .kb/investigations/2025-12-29-inv-create-opencode-plugin-orch-session.md
close_reason: Consolidated session-context.js and orch-session-autostart.ts into single orchestrator-session.ts plugin at ~/.config/opencode/plugin/. Shared isWorker() detection cached at init, both config (skill injection) and event (session auto-start) hooks preserved. Investigation files updated with lineage. Leave it Better: kn command not available, but learning noted in investigation history (cache detection at init for efficiency).

The usage stats shown as '40% 5h 25% wk personal' are cryptic. Users need to decipher what each number means.

- 40% = hourly usage
- 5h = hours until reset
- 25% = weekly usage  
- wk = weekly indicator
- personal = account name

- Add labels: '40% hourly | 25% weekly'
- Or use icons with tooltips
- Or restructure: '25% weekly (40% this hour) • resets in 5h'
- Show which limit is constraining (hourly vs weekly)

- cmd/orch/serve.go - usage API response

title: Consolidate artifact templates into kb-cli
description:
## Problem

Artifact templates are fragmented across multiple locations:
- orch-knowledge/skills/src/worker/{skill}/templates/
- ~/.claude/skills/worker/{skill}/templates/
- ~/.kb/templates/
- ~/.orch/templates/
- kb-cli hardcoded defaults

This causes:
- Drift between copies (e.g., investigation template has D.E.K.N. in some places, not others)
- Confusion about which is authoritative
- Templates in skill directories that skillc doesn't manage

## Solution

1. **kb-cli owns artifact templates** (investigation, decision, guide, research)
   - Update hardcoded defaults in kb-cli with current best versions
   - User overrides at ~/.kb/templates/
   - Add `kb create research` command if missing

2. **Remove templates from skill directories**
   - Delete orch-knowledge/skills/src/worker/investigation/templates/
   - Delete orch-knowledge/skills/src/worker/research/templates/
   - Delete ~/.claude/skills/worker/*/templates/

3. **orch-go owns spawn-time templates** (already correct)
   - SYNTHESIS.md, SPAWN_CONTEXT.md, etc. stay in .orch/templates/

4. **Sync ~/.kb/templates/ with kb-cli defaults**
   - Or remove user overrides if they match defaults

## Scope

| Template | Current Locations | Target |
|----------|------------------|--------|
| investigation.md | 5 places | kb-cli only |
| research.md | 2 places | kb-cli only |
| decision | 2 places | kb-cli only |
| guide | 1 place | kb-cli only |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] kb-cli hardcoded templates updated with D.E.K.N. + structured uncertainty
- [ ] `kb create research` works (add if missing)
- [ ] Templates removed from skill directories
- [ ] Skills reference `kb create` instead of embedding templates
- [ ] ~/.kb/templates/ cleaned up or synced

title: Epic: Dashboard 'Needs Attention' Redesign
description:
## Problem

The dashboard 'Needs Attention' section is broken in multiple ways:
- Shows noise, not actionable items
- 'Pending Reviews' with 72 items creates anxiety, not action
- 'Blocked issues' surfaces expected behavior (sequential deps) as problems
- No clear actions - items shown without verbs/commands

## Goal

Transform 'Needs Attention' from overwhelming backlog into focused decision surface.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Small actionable count (3-5 items max normally)
- [ ] Each item has a clear action verb
- [ ] Only surfaces items requiring human intervention
- [ ] Dismissed items don't resurface inappropriately

## Child Issues

Consolidates:
- orch-go-fqb8: Redesign for actionable decisions (broad scope)
- orch-go-t404: Blocked issues misleading (specific fix)
- orch-go-va5v: Replace 'Pending Reviews' with 'Recent Knowledge'

## Design Direction

From orch-go-fqb8:
```
Needs Attention (3)

🔴 BLOCKING: 2 agents at Phase: Complete need review
   → Click to complete

⚠️  DECISION NEEDED: 1 issue needs triage
   → Click to triage

📊 PATTERN: 'chrome launch' gap recurring (5x)
   → Consider: kn constrain
```

Categories prioritized: BLOCKING > DECISION NEEDED > PATTERNS
close_reason: Already completed: 🔴 BLOCKING: 2 agents at Phase: Complete need review

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: BLOCKED: This epic proposes solutions before understanding the problem. orch-go-39jx (Dashboard requirements investigation) should complete first to determine what 'Needs Attention' should actually surface. Design-First Gate applied.

title: Standardize binary installation location across CLI tools
description: Pattern: binaries get installed to both ~/bin/ and ~/go/bin/, causing PATH shadowing where stale binaries mask new ones. Hit with orch (stale binary), kb (stale binary). Investigate: standardize on one location, add staleness check, or make install scripts consistent across orch-go, kb-cli, bd.
close_reason: Investigation complete. Root cause: 3 of 6 CLI tools use go install (→ ~/go/bin) while PATH gives ~/go/bin priority over ~/bin. Recommendation: Standardize on orch-go Makefile pattern (build → copy to ~/bin → codesign). Follow-up issues needed for beads, skillc, agentlog. Constraint recorded via kn.

title: Active agent titles sometimes unhelpful (e.g. 'Orchestrator skill says')
description:
## Problem

Active agent cards show titles that aren't meaningful. Example: 'Orchestrator skill says' doesn't tell the user what the agent is actually doing.

## Evidence

Screenshot shows an active agent with title 'Orchestrator skill says' - this is the start of the task description but truncated unhelpfully.

## Root Cause

Title is derived from task description which may start with context rather than the actual task. For investigations spawned with questions, the question text becomes the title.

## Expected

Show a more useful title:
- Use beads issue title if available
- Fall back to skill + short task summary
- Consider extracting the actual task from longer descriptions

## Files
- web/src/routes/+page.svelte - AgentCard title display
- cmd/orch/serve.go - agent data sent to UI

title: Dashboard doesn't detect completion for untracked agents
description:
## Problem

Dashboard shows untracked agents (--no-track) as 'active' indefinitely because:
1. No beads issue exists → no Phase: Complete comment to check
2. Falls back to 10-minute activity timeout only

## Evidence

Agent orch-knowledge-untracked-1766690045 completed its work (committed to repo) but dashboard shows it as Active at 7m 25s.

## Solution

For agents without beads tracking, check alternative completion signals:
1. SYNTHESIS.md exists in workspace → completed (full tier)
2. .tier file says 'light' AND commits exist → completed (light tier)
3. OpenCode session is_processing=false for >2 minutes → idle

## Related

Fix orch-go-k08g only addressed tracked agents with beads Phase: Complete comments.
close_reason: Added workspace SYNTHESIS.md check as fallback for untracked agent completion detection. Tests pass, implementation follows existing patterns.

title: Replace duplicate agent counts with usage progress bars in stats bar
description:
## Problem

Stats bar shows active/recent/archive counts that are already visible in Swarm Map section headers. This wastes horizontal space (critical at 666px width constraint).

## Solution

Replace agent count section with usage progress bars:
- Before: `1 active | 25 recent | 184 archive | 0 errors`
- After: `[████░░] 64% hr ↻5hr | [██░░░░] 28% wk | 0 errors`

## Design

Progress bars for hourly and weekly usage:
- Green (<50%), Yellow (50-80%), Red (>80%)
- Show reset time for hourly (the constraining limit)
- Compact format to fit 666px width

## Keep
- 0 errors (not duplicated)
- focused indicator
- beads ready/blocked  
- servers status
- @personal account indicator

## Remove
- active count (shown in Active section header)
- recent count (shown in Recent section header)
- archive count (shown in Archive section header)

## Files
- web/src/routes/+page.svelte - stats bar layout
close_reason: Replaced duplicate active/recent/archive counts with usage progress bars showing hourly and weekly limits with reset times. Extended /api/usage to include reset times. Commit e708c66.

title: Dashboard shows stale/dead agents after OpenCode restart - doesn't clear old sessions
description: After restarting OpenCode, the dashboard continues showing old dead agents as 'Active Agents' with 'Dead - needs attention' status. The CLI (orch status) shows correct data, but the dashboard at localhost:5188 shows 4 stale agents that should have been cleared. This appears to be a sync issue between the dashboard's SSE connection and the registry state after OpenCode restarts.
close_reason: Investigation found threshold already aligned (3 min). The 'stale agents' shown on dashboard are from OpenCode's session persistence - dead sessions are intentionally shown in Active section for user attention. Not a bug, working as designed.

title: Dashboard artifact viewer doesn't find cross-project investigation files
description:
When viewing a completed agent from another project (e.g., pw-4014 from price-watch while dashboard runs from orch-go), the Investigation tab fails to load with 404.

## Root Cause
Artifact API only searches current project's directories:
- .orch/workspace/{name}/SYNTHESIS.md
- .kb/investigations/

But cross-project agents have their artifacts in the source project's directories.

## Example
Agent: og-inv-kb-setup-audit-30dec [pw-4014]
Investigation at: price-watch/.kb/investigations/2025-12-30-inv-kb-setup-audit-price-watch.md
API searches: orch-go/.kb/investigations/ (wrong project)

## Possible Solutions
1. Store PROJECT_DIR in agent metadata, use it to resolve artifact paths
2. Parse beads ID prefix (pw- vs orch-go-) to infer project
3. Search all known project directories for artifacts

## Related
- Fixed workspace ID suffix bug: 32cf0792
- Cross-project agent visibility already works for status/phase
close_reason: Fixed cross-project investigation/decision file discovery

title: Epic: Amnesia-Resilient Artifact Architecture
description:
Design and implement coherent artifact architecture across orchestration ecosystem. Goal: Any fresh Claude can resume work with zero context loss.

Themes identified:
1. Workspace lifecycle (archival, retention, cleanup)
2. Knowledge flow (project → global promotion, investigation → decision)
3. Orchestrator session boundaries (handoffs, synthesis timing)
4. Cross-artifact relationships (beads ↔ kb ↔ workspace)
5. Multi-agent coordination (synthesis, conflict detection)
6. Failure mode capture (post-mortems, structured failure analysis)

Success criteria: Minimal artifact set with explicit relationships that fully captures system state.

Foundation: .kb/investigations/2025-12-21-design-deep-pattern-analysis-orchestration-artifacts.md

title: Investigation: Workspace lifecycle and archival strategy

- Should there be .orch/archive/ for completed agent syntheses?
- What's the retention policy - keep all? successful only? time-based?
- How does archival interact with kb context discovery?

Context: Currently SYNTHESIS.md is deleted with workspace cleanup, losing session summaries. This may violate session amnesia principle - future Claude can't learn from past agent work patterns.

title: Design: Reflection checkpoint pattern for agent sessions
description:
Pattern observed: Agent's most valuable output came from interactive follow-up ('what else do you see?') not autonomous completion.

The pattern: Autonomous work → Human probe → Deeper synthesis → Richer output

Encode via:
1. Skill-level: Add reflection phase before completion
2. Spawn-level: --interactive flag for pause-before-complete
3. Protocol-level: SPAWN_CONTEXT template update
4. Artifact-level: Require 'unexplored questions' section in SYNTHESIS.md

Key insight: Agents completing autonomously is efficient but may leave value on the table. Most generative moments happen at boundary between agent work and human judgment.

Related: SESSION_HANDOFF.md pattern captures 'what next session should explore'

Parent: orch-go-4kwt

title: Active agents constantly reorder as they update, causing visual instability
description:
## Problem

Active agents in the Swarm Map fight for position as they update, causing jarring visual jumps. The gold-highlighted active cards rapidly swap positions.

## Evidence

Screenshot shows 4 active agents. As each updates (every few seconds), they jump around in the grid.

## Expected

Active agents should maintain stable positions. Either:
- Sort once on initial render, then maintain position
- Sort by a stable key (beads ID, spawn time) rather than last update time

## Files
- web/src/routes/+page.svelte - agent grid rendering and sorting logic
close_reason: Fixed active agents grid reordering by using spawned_at (stable) instead of updated_at (volatile) for secondary sort. Removed dead code (unused filteredAgents). All tests pass.

title: [orch-go] investigation: concurrent test beta
Epic: Orchestrator behavioral self-correction
description:
Enable orchestrator to detect and correct repeated behavioral mistakes without human intervention.

Root cause: Tool outcomes are ephemeral - no feedback loop exists for 'you tried X 3 times with empty result'.

Current mechanisms track knowledge state (kn, kb, orch learn) but not action outcomes. Self-correction requires: action → outcome → pattern detection → adjustment.

Implementation sequence:
1. Action logging subsystem (~/.orch/action-log.jsonl)
2. Pattern analyzer (extend learning.go)
3. Surfacing mechanism (orch patterns or SessionStart injection)

See investigation: .kb/investigations/2025-12-27-inv-orchestrator-self-correction-mechanisms-problem.md

title: Epic: Migrate skills to skillc-managed structure
description:
## Goal

Migrate ~/.claude/skills/ from manual SKILL.md files to skillc-managed .skillc/ sources, enabling modular skill development with dependency resolution and automated compilation.

## Context

- skillc exists at ~/Documents/personal/skillc with production-ready CLAUDE.md compilation
- orch-knowledge repo no longer exists (confirmed)
- Current skills have mixed structures: src/ (modular, 2 skills) vs manual SKILL.md (rest)
- skillc already supports custom output filenames (can output SKILL.md)

## Scope

**In scope:**
- Enhance skillc manifest for SKILL.md-specific patterns (frontmatter handling)
- Pilot migration of one skill to validate the pattern
- Convert existing modular skills (feature-impl, codebase-audit) to .skillc/
- Documentation of the migration pattern
- Verification that orch spawn works with compiled SKILL.md

**Out of scope:**
- Converting all monolithic skills (optional, low-value)
- Changes to orch-go pkg/skills/loader.go (read-only, no changes needed)
- Recursive skill dependencies (future enhancement)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] skillc can compile .skillc/ → SKILL.md with valid YAML frontmatter
- [ ] orch spawn works with skillc-compiled SKILL.md files
- [ ] At least 3 skills migrated (investigation, feature-impl, codebase-audit)
- [ ] Migration pattern documented for future skill authors

## Investigation Reference

See: orch-go/.kb/investigations/2025-12-22-inv-epic-replace-orch-knowledge-skillc.md
close_reason:
Epic complete. All success criteria met:
- skillc compiles .skillc/ → SKILL.md with valid frontmatter (type: skill support added)
- orch spawn works with skillc-compiled SKILL.md files (verified)
- 3 skills migrated: investigation, feature-impl, codebase-audit
- Migration pattern documented at ~/orch-knowledge/docs/skillc-skill-migration.md

Sources now in orch-knowledge, deployed to ~/.claude/skills/. Old src/ structures removed.

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: DESIGN UPDATE: Skills should NOT have .skillc/ sources in ~/.claude/skills/ (deploy target). Correct pattern: sources in versioned repo (orch-knowledge or dedicated skills repo), deploy to ~/.claude/skills/ via 'skillc deploy --target'. This enables version control, review workflow, and machine-independent skill development. Child tasks need updating to reflect this.

title: Enhance skillc for SKILL.md frontmatter handling
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-4ztg: Epic: Migrate skills to skillc-managed structure

## Problem

skillc currently adds header comments at the start of compiled output:
```markdown
<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by skillc -->
<!-- Source: .skillc -->
...
```

SKILL.md files require YAML frontmatter at line 1:
```yaml
---
name: investigation
skill-type: procedure
...
---
```

If skillc header appears first, YAML parsing fails.

## Task

Modify skillc to handle SKILL.md compilation:
1. Add `type: skill` option in skill.yaml manifest
2. When type=skill: emit frontmatter first, header comments after
3. Support explicit frontmatter source file (e.g., frontmatter.yaml → merged into output)

## Alternative Approaches

- Option A: type field switches header placement
- Option B: Explicit frontmatter.yaml file in sources
- Option C: Detect output filename ends with SKILL.md, auto-adjust

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] skill.yaml can specify type: skill
- [ ] Compiled SKILL.md has frontmatter at line 1
- [ ] orch-go ParseSkillMetadata() parses compiled output correctly
- [ ] Existing CLAUDE.md compilation unchanged (backward compatible)

## Technical Notes

- See skillc/pkg/compiler/compiler.go:91 for header generation
- See orch-go/pkg/skills/loader.go:103-125 for ParseSkillMetadata
close_reason: Batch close from orch review

title: [orch-go] investigation: timing validation run 3
orch status still slow: getCompletionsForReview runs VerifyCompletionFull on 303 workspaces
description:
## Problem

`orch status` is still 1m26s despite workspace cache fix (c4d21d8d). JSON mode is fast (1s) but text output is slow.

## Root Cause

`getCompletionsForReview()` in cmd/orch/review.go:138 runs `verify.VerifyCompletionFull()` for EVERY workspace with SYNTHESIS.md:
- 303 workspaces with SYNTHESIS.md
- Each VerifyCompletionFull does beads lookups, file reads, etc.
- Called from `GetArchitectRecommendationsSurface()` which is called in text output mode

## Evidence

```
time orch status --json  # 1.0s (fast)
time orch status         # 1m26s (slow)
find .orch/workspace -name 'SYNTHESIS.md' | wc -l  # 303
```

## Fix Options

1. **Cache completions** - Don't re-verify on every status call
2. **Lazy load** - Only verify when explicitly requested 
3. **Skip old workspaces** - Only verify recent (e.g., <24h old)
4. **Move to API** - Have orch serve cache this data
close_reason: Zombie issue reconciled - no active agent found

comments.text: Phase: Implementing - Creating getCompletionsForSurfacing() to skip verification for architect recommendations

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Phase: Complete - Fixed orch status performance from 1m26s to 1.1s by creating getCompletionsForSurfacing() that skips expensive verification. GetArchitectRecommendationsSurface and GetArchitectRecommendationCount now use the lightweight function.

This causes 'silent failures' when orchestrator runs orch complete - the issue appears closed but isn't.

title: Explore tradeoffs for orch-go opencode integration.
description: Explore tradeoffs for orch-go opencode integration. Current problem: orch-go uses 'opencode run --attach {server} --title {name} {prompt}' which doesn't render TUI correctly in tmux. Python orch-cli uses standalone mode: 'opencode {project_dir}' then waits for TUI ready, then types prompt via send-keys. Options to explore: 1) Match Python's standalone approach, 2) Fix attach mode to work with TUI, 3) Hybrid approach, 4) Other alternatives. Consider: session management, server coordination, TUI rendering, complexity, reliability, feature parity with Python orch-cli.

title: Update tmux spawn to use attach mode for dual TUI+API access
description:
## Problem

Currently `orch spawn --tmux` uses standalone mode (`opencode /path --model X`) which creates an ephemeral server on a random port. Sessions created in standalone mode are NOT visible to the main OpenCode server at `:4096`, so:

- No `session_id` captured in registry
- Cannot interact via HTTP API (`orch send`, `orch tail` fall back to tmux capture)
- No structured message access
- Monitoring/status checks require tmux scraping

This means tmux spawns are "human-friendly" (TUI visible) but NOT "AI-friendly" (no API access).

## Solution

Switch from standalone mode to attach mode:

```go
// OLD (standalone - ephemeral server)
opencode /path/to/project --model X

// NEW (attach - shared server)  
opencode attach http://127.0.0.1:4096 --dir /path/to/project --model X
```

With attach mode + the OpenCode fix (already applied locally):
1. TUI connects to shared server at `:4096`
2. Sessions created with correct `directory` (via `x-opencode-directory` header)
3. Sessions visible in API immediately
4. Can capture `session_id` and store in registry
5. Full API access: messages, status, send prompts

## Implementation Details

### 1. Update `pkg/tmux/tmux.go`

Change `BuildStandaloneCommand()` to `BuildAttachCommand()`:

```go
type AttachConfig struct {
    ServerURL  string // http://127.0.0.1:4096
    ProjectDir string
    Model      string
    SessionID  string // optional: continue existing session
}

func BuildAttachCommand(cfg *AttachConfig) string {
    cmd := fmt.Sprintf("opencode attach %s --dir %s", 
        cfg.ServerURL, 
        shlex.Quote(cfg.ProjectDir))
    if cfg.Model != "" {
        cmd += fmt.Sprintf(" --model %s", shlex.Quote(cfg.Model))
    }
    if cfg.SessionID != "" {
        cmd += fmt.Sprintf(" --session %s", cfg.SessionID)
    }
    return cmd
}
```

### 2. Update `cmd/orch/main.go` `runSpawnTmux()`

After TUI is ready and prompt is sent:
1. Query `/session` API with `x-opencode-directory` header
2. Find most recent session for this project
3. Store `session_id` in registry

```go
// After SendPromptAfterReady succeeds
sessionID, err := findRecentSession(serverURL, cfg.ProjectDir)
if err != nil {
    // Log warning but don't fail - tmux fallback still works
    log.Printf("Warning: could not capture session_id: %v", err)
}

// Update registry with session_id
agent.SessionID = sessionID
```

### 3. Update registry usage

Commands that currently fall back to tmux when `session_id` is missing can now use API:
- `orch tail` - use `/session/{id}/message` 
- `orch send` - use `/session/{id}/prompt`
- `orch question` - parse from API messages

## Dependencies

**OpenCode fix required:** The SDK must pass `directory: process.cwd()` to API calls.

Fix already applied locally at:
`opencode/packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/context/sdk.tsx`

```typescript
const sdk = createOpencodeClient({
  baseUrl: props.url,
  signal: abort.signal,
  directory: process.cwd(),  // THIS LINE ADDED
})
```

This fix needs to be:
1. Committed to opencode repo
2. Released as new version
3. Or: use locally built opencode binary

## Testing

1. `orch spawn --tmux investigation "test"` creates session
2. Session visible via: `curl http://127.0.0.1:4096/session -H "x-opencode-directory: $PWD"`
3. `session_id` present in registry: `cat ~/.orch/agent-registry.json | jq '.agents[-1].session_id'`
4. `orch tail <id>` uses API (not tmux fallback)
5. `orch send <id> "message"` uses API

## Evidence

Tested attach mode with fixed OpenCode locally:

```bash
# Session created with correct directory
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:4096/session \
  -H "x-opencode-directory: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go" \
  | jq 'sort_by(.time.created) | last'

# Output:
{
  "id": "ses_4bf7f116bffeaVAetTEdKTtsD4",
  "title": "New session - 2025-12-21T10:42:30.164Z",
  "dir": "/Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go",
  ...
}

# Can fetch messages via API
curl -s "http://127.0.0.1:4096/session/ses_4bf7f116bffeaVAetTEdKTtsD4/message" \
  -H "x-opencode-directory: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go"
# Returns structured message history
```

## Related

- Investigation: `.kb/investigations/2025-12-21-inv-tmux-spawn-killed.md`
- Python orch-cli gave up on attach mode due to this bug: `src/orch/spawn.py:959-960`
- OpenCode source: `packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/context/sdk.tsx`

title: orch next: Synthesize prioritized work recommendations
description:
Combine multiple signals into ranked 'what to work on next' recommendations.

Current state: Orchestrator must manually run and synthesize:
- bd ready (available work)
- orch patterns (problem areas)
- orch focus (strategic alignment)
- orch learn (knowledge gaps)

Desired state: `orch next` synthesizes these into prioritized recommendations:
1. Critical blockers (patterns showing active failures)
2. Focus-aligned ready work
3. High-value maintenance (recurring patterns worth fixing)
4. Strategic backlog

Example output:
```
orch next

RECOMMENDED NEXT ACTIONS (aligned with focus: 'Ship snap MVP')

1. [BLOCKER] orch-go-xyz has failed 3x in last 24h - spawn systematic-debugging
2. [FOCUS] orch-go-abc ready - implements snap auth (P1)
3. [FOCUS] orch-go-def ready - snap API endpoint (P2)
4. [MAINTENANCE] 'chrome launch' gap occurred 5x - consider kn constrain
5. [BACKLOG] orch-go-ghi ready - consolidation phase 1 (P2, not focus-aligned)
```

Key behaviors:
- Filter patterns to active/open issues only
- Weight by focus alignment
- Surface blockers before new work
- Show reasoning for prioritization

title: Fix: tmux spawn should not use --format json
description:
## Problem

When spawning in tmux mode, the agent runs with `--format json` which outputs raw JSON instead of OpenCode's interactive TUI.

## Current Behavior
```
opencode run --attach ... --format json --title ... "prompt"
# Outputs raw JSON lines to terminal
```

## Expected Behavior
```
opencode run --attach ... --title ... "prompt"
# Shows OpenCode's normal TUI
```

## Root Cause
`pkg/tmux/tmux.go:BuildSpawnCommand()` hardcodes `--format json`.

## Fix
- Remove `--format json` from tmux spawn command

title: kb reflect needs semantic clustering - group by topic not keyword
description:
**From investigation:** 2025-12-28-inv-knowledge-fragmentation-433-investigations-days.md

**Problem:** kb reflect --type synthesis groups by word frequency ('add' = 41, 'orch' = 36) not conceptual topic. This makes synthesis suggestions unhelpful - 41 'add' investigations aren't related, they just share a word.

**Recommendation:** Cluster by semantic topic (dashboard, headless, daemon) not keyword. Simple topic extraction from titles may suffice - don't over-engineer.

**Success criteria:** kb reflect --type synthesis shows semantic topics (dashboard UX, spawn modes, daemon lifecycle) not keyword groups (add, implement, orch)

title: Add orch init command for project scaffolding
description:
From inv-scope-orch-init investigation:

**Problem:** New projects need manual setup of .beads/, .kb/, .orch/, CLAUDE.md. Inconsistent across repos.

**Solution:** `orch init` command that:
1. Creates directory structure (.beads/, .kb/investigations/, .orch/workspace/, .orch/templates/)
2. Copies SYNTHESIS.md template
3. Creates minimal CLAUDE.md if missing
4. Runs `bd init` if beads not initialized
5. Adds to ~/.orch/projects.yaml for cross-project tracking

**Flags:**
- `--minimal` - just directories, no templates
- `--force` - overwrite existing

**Also includes:**
- Port allocation registry integration
- Tmuxinator config generation

Ref: .kb/investigations/2025-12-21-inv-scope-orch-init-project-standardization.md

description: Investigation files become stale after implementation completes. Root cause options: (1) investigations don't link to beads issues that implement their recommendations, (2) no lifecycle to mark investigations as 'implemented', (3) kb context doesn't check if related beads issues are closed. Fix: investigations should either link to implementing issue OR have a status field that gets updated when work completes.

## Investigation Update
- **Root Cause:** opencode TUI initializes default 'Build' agent (Opus 4.5) before session flags, triggering Anthropic API key check even when Gemini is requested.
- **Fix:** Switched spawn logic to use 'opencode run' mode. This passes model and prompt atomically, bypassing TUI agent defaults.

- **Status:** Fix implemented in orch-go and deployed.

title: orch status hangs/crashes with exit code 137 (SIGKILL)
description:
## Problem

`orch status` and `orch complete` commands hang and get killed (exit 137 = SIGKILL).

## Evidence

```bash
~/bin/orch status 2>&1 || echo "exit code: $?"
# Output: exit code: 137

timeout 10 ~/bin/orch complete orch-go-9e15.3 2>&1
# Output: Timed out or failed
```

## Impact

- Can't use CLI to check agent status
- Can't use CLI to complete agents
- Have to manually close via `bd close`

## Likely cause

Something in the status/complete command is blocking indefinitely - possibly:
- HTTP request to OpenCode hanging
- File lock contention
- Infinite loop in reconciliation
close_reason: Root cause: stale binary at ~/bin/orch. Fixed by force reinstalling from cmd/orch build. Not a code bug.

title: Phase 3: beads abstraction layer (Month 4)
description:
## Scope
Create pkg/beads/client.go interface to reduce direct bd CLI calls and enable mock testing.

## Tasks
1. Audit current bd CLI calls in orch-go codebase
2. Design beads client interface (create, close, comment, list, etc.)
3. Implement CLI-backed client (wraps bd commands)
4. Add mock implementation for testing
5. Refactor orch-go commands to use client interface
6. Add integration tests with mock beads

## Why Abstraction Needed
- beads is external dependency (stevey's project)
- Direct CLI coupling makes testing hard
- API stability not guaranteed
- Enables potential direct beads-lib integration later

## Success Criteria
- pkg/beads/client.go interface exists
- All orch-go commands use client interface
- Mock client enables unit testing without bd binary

title: Fix headless spawn race condition - add message verification after SendPrompt
description: Root cause identified in orch-go-tulk investigation: SendPrompt is called immediately after CreateSession with no delay/verification in headless mode, while tmux mode has 1-second delay. Fix: After calling SendPrompt, poll GetMessages until message count > 0 (or timeout after 5s). If verification fails, retry SendPrompt once. See: .kb/investigations/2025-12-30-inv-headless-spawn-silent-failures-agents.md
close_reason: Fixed headless spawn race condition by adding message verification after SendPrompt. WaitForMessage polls GetMessages until count > 0 (5s timeout, 300ms interval). SendPromptWithVerification retries once if verification fails. All unit tests passing. Changes committed in 155e1771.

title: Add multi-provider model routing (OpenRouter/DeepSeek)
description:
## Problem

When trying to use non-Claude models (Gemini 2.5 Flash, DeepSeek), the system doesn't handle them cleanly. Model aliases only cover Anthropic models currently.

## Solution

Add model aliases for additional providers to `pkg/model/model.go`:

### Phase 1: Gemini aliases
- `flash` → `google/gemini-2.5-flash-preview-05-20`
- `pro` → `google/gemini-2.5-pro-preview-05-06`

### Phase 2: DeepSeek aliases (if API key configured)
- `deepseek` → `deepseek/deepseek-chat`
- `deepseek-r1` → `deepseek/deepseek-reasoner`

### Phase 3: OpenRouter passthrough
- `openrouter/\*` format passthrough

## Evidence

- Investigation: `.kb/investigations/2025-12-24-inv-model-provider-architecture-orch-vs.md`
- Current aliases in `pkg/model/model.go:46-83`
- Architecture confirmed: orch owns aliases, OpenCode owns auth

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `orch spawn --model flash` works with Gemini
- [ ] `orch spawn --model deepseek` works (if API key configured)
- [ ] Existing Claude aliases unchanged

title: Detect and handle agents that exhaust context with uncommitted work
description:
## Problem

Agent orch-go-wj8n (spawn_cmd.go extraction) completed the implementation but exhausted context (76.6K tokens, 5 messages) before committing or writing SYNTHESIS.md. The work was done but 'landing' was incomplete.

## Observed Pattern

1. Agent does implementation work (files created/modified, tests pass)
2. Agent exhausts context before 'Leave it Better' phase
3. Session goes idle with uncommitted changes
4. Orchestrator has to manually commit and close

## Evidence

- orch-go-wj8n: 76.6K tokens, spawn_cmd.go created but no commit
- orch-go-poit: 21.7K tokens, completed normally (smaller task)

## Potential Solutions

1. **Detection**: Alert when agent goes idle with uncommitted changes in working directory
2. **Checkpointing**: Add intermediate commits during implementation (not just at end)
3. **Scope management**: Warn or split tasks estimated to exceed context budget
4. **Graceful degradation**: If context pressure detected, prioritize commit over synthesis

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] System detects agents idle with uncommitted work
- [ ] Orchestrator is alerted to incomplete landings
- [ ] Consider: automatic recovery (commit on behalf of agent)
close_reason: Context exhaustion detection: RISK column with uncommitted work detection

title: kb reflect should auto-create synthesis issues at threshold
description:
When kb reflect detects 10+ investigations on a topic, auto-create a beads issue:
- Title: 'Synthesize {topic} investigations ({count})'
- Label: triage:review (orchestrator decides priority)
- Link to relevant investigations
- Description includes kb chronicle output snippet

This connects reflection output to the work pipeline without requiring manual initiative. Preserves orchestrator judgment (they prioritize) while creating pressure (issue exists, visible, doesn't disappear).

Implements: Gate Over Remind principle
Implements: Pressure Over Compensation principle

Related: kb-reflect skill exists for the actual synthesis work.

title: Fix launchd plist PATH to include homebrew and npm paths
description:
orch servers init generates plists with incomplete PATH. Servers fail to start because go/npm not found.

Current PATH in plist:
/Users/dylanconlin/bin:/Users/dylanconlin/.local/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin

Missing:
- /opt/homebrew/bin (go)
- ~/.npm-global/bin (npm)

Fix: Detect actual binary paths during plist generation, or inherit from current shell PATH.

Related: cmd/orch/servers.go, pkg/servers/detect.go
close_reason: Fixed launchd plist PATH inheritance. DefaultPlistOptions() now uses os.Getenv("PATH") instead of hardcoded path, ensuring npm/go are found at /opt/homebrew/bin and ~/.asdf/shims. All tests passing.

title: orch serve CPU runaway - deeper fix needed
description: Previous fix (ed772bac - removing IsProcessing HTTP calls, 500ms debounce) didn't hold. CPU hit 124% again after ~15 min. Need deeper investigation: goroutine leaks, unbounded SSE client connections, missing cleanup on disconnect, or other feedback loops. Consider pprof profiling. Blocking dashboard usability.
close_reason: Fixed CPU runaway: implemented workspace cache reducing O(n*m) file ops to O(m)+O(n). 5-minute stress test shows CPU at 4-7% (down from 124%). Added pprof at /debug/pprof/ for diagnostics. Investigation and SYNTHESIS.md committed.

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Phase: Planning - Reading prior decision and daemon/serve code to understand previous fix attempt

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: investigation_path: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/investigations/2025-12-25-inv-orch-serve-cpu-runaway-recurring.md

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Phase: Root Cause Investigation - Analyzing SSE handlers, polling loops, and connection cleanup for CPU runaway causes

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Phase: Pattern Analysis - Root cause identified: handleAgents scans 466 workspace directories on EVERY request. With 500ms SSE debounce and frequent events, this creates massive I/O overhead leading to CPU runaway.

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Phase: Implementing - Fix committed: buildWorkspaceCache() scans 466 directories ONCE per request instead of N*466 scans where N = number of agents. Also added pprof endpoint at /debug/pprof/ for future diagnostics.

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Phase: Complete - Fixed CPU runaway: implemented workspace cache reducing O(n*m) file ops to O(m)+O(n). 5-minute stress test shows CPU at 4-7% (down from 124%). Added pprof at /debug/pprof/ for diagnostics. Investigation and SYNTHESIS.md committed.

title: Add periodic kb reflect to daemon
description:
Daemon should run kb reflect --type synthesis --create-issue periodically (hourly?) to auto-surface synthesis opportunities.

This closes the automation loop:
- Daemon runs kb reflect
- kb reflect creates triage:review issues for 10+ investigation topics
- Orchestrator sees issues in bd ready
- Orchestrator (or daemon) spawns kb-reflect agent
- Agent produces decisions/archives

Implementation options:
1. Add to daemon poll loop with cycle counter (every 60 cycles = hourly)
2. Separate goroutine with ticker
3. New daemon subcommand: orch daemon reflect

Should probably be configurable (enable/disable, frequency) in ~/.orch/config.yaml

Epic: Harden bd CLI integration against load-related failures
description:
After dashboard feature increased bd CLI usage 10x, we hit multiple bugs: daemon race condition, unbounded concurrency, CLI flag misuse. This epic covers merging existing fixes and adding structural improvements to prevent recurrence.

## Context
- Dashboard polls every 2-5 seconds, batches 10-50+ agents
- 19 exec.Command bd calls throughout codebase
- Beads daemon fix on branch, not merged to main

## Success Criteria
- Daemon fix merged to beads main
- All batch bd operations have concurrency limits
- Load testing validates 50+ agent scenarios
- No CPU spikes under normal dashboard usage
close_reason: All 8 children complete - beads RPC client implemented, load testing done, concurrency audited

title: Design: Go client for beads vs shell exec
description:
Currently shell out to bd CLI 19 times. This is fragile - no type safety, CLI flag surprises, process overhead, hard to mock.

## Options to evaluate
1. Keep shelling out + defensive wrappers
2. Import beads as Go module
3. beads HTTP API
4. Shared SQLite access

## Deliverable
Recommendation with tradeoffs

title: Implement pkg/beads Go RPC client
description:
Create pkg/beads/client.go with connection management to beads daemon socket.

## Context
Replacing bd CLI subprocess calls with native RPC to eliminate process overhead, get type safety, and avoid CLI quirks.

## Implementation
- Pattern after beads internal/rpc/client.go
- Connect to .beads/bd.sock Unix domain socket
- Include health checks, reconnect logic
- Types from beads/internal/rpc/protocol.go
- Support 7 operations: ready, show, list, stats, comments, close, create

## Acceptance Criteria
- Client can connect to beads daemon
- All 7 operations work
- Fallback behavior when daemon unavailable
- Unit tests with mock socket
close_reason: Implemented pkg/beads Go RPC client with 7 operations (ready, show, list, stats, comments, close, create), Unix socket connection to .beads/bd.sock, health check on connect, reconnect logic, and CLI fallback functions. All 14 tests pass, full test suite passes. Committed as 9657cd9.

title: Merge beads daemon race condition fix to main
description:
CRITICAL: The daemon race fix (commit aa401428) is on fix-repo-empty-config branch, 15 commits ahead of main. If anyone rebuilds bd from main, CPU spike bug returns.

## What to do
1. cd ~/Documents/personal/beads
2. git checkout main
3. git merge fix-repo-empty-config (or cherry-pick aa401428)
4. Rebuild and install bd

## The fix
killDuplicateDaemons() sanity check after acquiring flock - prevents process accumulation.

## Risk if not done
High - next bd rebuild from main reintroduces CPU overload bug.
close_reason: Merged fix-repo-empty-config to main (15 commits including aa401428 daemon race fix). Fixed minor build issue (SourceRepo field) and rebuilt bd v0.33.2.

title: Create pkg/beads abstraction layer (Phase 3)
description:
## Context

Ecosystem audit follow-up (orch-go-tyvz, orch-go-cnq5) confirmed beads should remain as external dependency with abstraction layer to isolate API changes and enable testing.

## Current State

- 19 exec.Command calls across orch-go using 7 bd commands
- Direct CLI calls scattered in daemon, verify, serve, spawn packages
- No mock testing possible for beads interactions

## Implementation

### 1. pkg/beads/interface.go
Define interface for the 7 commands used:
- `Comment(issueID, text string)` - Add progress comment
- `Comments(issueID string)` - Get comments
- `Create(title, desc string, opts CreateOpts)` - Create issue
- `List(opts ListOpts)` - Query issues
- `Ready()` - Get ready queue
- `Show(issueID string)` - Get issue details
- `Stats()` - Dashboard stats

### 2. pkg/beads/client.go
Implement interface via bd CLI exec.Command calls.

### 3. pkg/beads/mock.go
Mock implementation for unit testing.

### 4. Migrate consumers
- pkg/daemon/ - Ready queue polling
- pkg/verify/ - Comment and issue checks
- cmd/orch/serve.go - Stats API
- pkg/spawn/ - Issue context loading

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Zero direct exec.Command("bd", ...) calls outside pkg/beads
- [ ] daemon package has mock-based tests
- [ ] verify package has mock-based tests
- [ ] All existing functionality preserved

## References

- .kb/investigations/2025-12-24-inv-follow-up-ecosystem-audit-orch.md
- .kb/investigations/2025-12-24-inv-addendum-ecosystem-audit-opencode.md
close_reason: pkg/beads abstraction layer implemented - backend refactor, no UI changes

title: Enhanced error visibility for headless spawns (optional)
description:
## Context

Optional enhancement identified during headless readiness investigation (orch-go-0r2q).

Current state: Basic error handling exists - HTTP API errors propagate, SSE reconnects automatically.

Gap: User messaging for spawn failures could be clearer, retry logic not implemented.

## Task

Enhance error visibility and recovery for headless spawn mode.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Spawn failures show clear error messages (not just generic HTTP errors)
- [ ] SSE connection failures logged and visible to user
- [ ] Optional: Retry logic for transient failures (network issues)
- [ ] Optional: Better error recovery guidance ("try again", "check OpenCode server")

## Priority

Low - current error handling is functional. This is polish/UX improvement, not blocker.

## Related

- Epic orch-go-9e15 (Make headless default) - not blocked by this
- Investigation .kb/investigations/2025-12-22-inv-headless-spawn-mode-readiness-what.md
close_reason: Added SpawnError types with clear messages and recovery guidance, retry logic for transient failures (3 attempts with exponential backoff), comprehensive tests. All acceptance criteria met.

title: orch complete should check for unfiled discovered work in investigations
description:
**Problem:** Investigations complete with 'Discovered Work Check' tables listing follow-up issues to create, but those issues never get filed. Knowledge evaporates.

**Evidence:** 2025-12-28-inv-knowledge-fragmentation-433-investigations-days.md had 3 recommendations in Discovered Work Check table, issue was closed, none were created. Only found 2 days later by accident.

**Recommendation:** Add verification step to orch complete for investigation skill:
1. Parse investigation file for 'Discovered Work Check' or similar section
2. If found with 'Create beads issue' actions, warn: 'Investigation has N unfiled discovered work items'
3. Either block completion or prompt orchestrator to file them

**Design question (applying Design-First Gate):** Why do investigations have a Discovered Work section that's not enforced? Should discovered work auto-create issues, or is the human review valuable?

**Success criteria:** Investigations with discovered work can't silently close without issues being filed.
close_reason: Verified: Tests pass (19 tests in pkg/verify). Discovered work gate implemented in pkg/verify/discovered_work.go. Commit c4e117a7.

title: Investigate model flexibility and arbitrage for orch-go
description:
Port model flexibility and account management from Python orch-cli to orch-go, with Gemini support baked in.

## Scope

1. **Model selection on spawn**
   - Add `--model` flag to `orch spawn`
   - Support provider format: `anthropic/opus`, `gemini/gemini-2.5-flash`
   - Add model aliases: `opus`, `sonnet`, `haiku`, `flash`
   - Pass model to OpenCode via `opencode run --model`

2. **Account management**
   - Add `orch account` command (save/switch/list/remove)
   - Read/write `~/.orch/accounts.yaml` (same format as Python)
   - Token sources: OpenCode auth file, macOS Keychain
   - Auto-switch when rate-limited (future)

3. **Usage tracking**
   - Add `orch usage` command (port from Python)
   - Support multi-account usage display
   - Warning thresholds for rate limits

## Reference
- Python implementation: `orch-cli/src/orch/accounts.py`, `usage.py`, `backends/opencode.py`
- Model resolution: `opencode.py:140-168` (OPENCODE_MODEL_ALIASES)
- Config format: `~/.orch/accounts.yaml`

## Why
Rate-limiting pain: spawning workers maxed out Claude Max limits. Needed Gemini fallback but spawn failed due to Anthropic API key assumption. This enables orchestrator to manage model/account selection.

title: Epic: Make headless spawn mode the default
description:
## Goal

Make headless spawn mode the default for orch-go, with tmux as opt-in via --tmux flag.

## Context

Investigation (.kb/investigations/2025-12-22-inv-headless-spawn-mode-readiness-what.md) verified all requirements are met:
- ✅ Status detection works for both modes
- ✅ Completion detection via SSE monitor
- ✅ Wait command works via beads comments
- ✅ Error handling exists at HTTP API layer
- ✅ User visibility equivalent to tmux

Headless is production-ready. Daemon already uses it exclusively. Only work needed is flipping the default and updating documentation.

## Scope

**In Scope:**
- Change default spawn mode from tmux to headless
- Add --tmux flag for opt-in tmux mode
- Update CLAUDE.md documentation (project and global)
- Update command help text and examples
- Update orchestrator skill documentation

**Out of Scope:**
- Enhanced error visibility (retry logic, better messaging) - can be separate epic
- Daemon integration testing - already designed for headless
- Breaking changes to existing spawns - backward compatibility maintained

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `orch spawn SKILL "task"` spawns headless by default
- [ ] `orch spawn --tmux SKILL "task"` spawns in tmux window
- [ ] All documentation reflects new default
- [ ] Orchestrator skill references updated
- [ ] No breaking changes to existing workflows
close_reason: All 4 children complete - headless is now default spawn mode

title: Stats bar requires horizontal scroll at 666px width - critical info hidden
description:
## Problem

At 666px width (half MacBook Pro screen), the stats bar overflows and requires horizontal scrolling. Critical info like beads ready count (173) and blocked count (17) are cut off.

## Evidence

Screenshot shows stats bar at 666px with horizontal scroll bar. Items visible:
- archive, 0 errors, focused, 3/24 servers, 173 ready, (17 blocked)
But the 'D' button is cut off, indicating more content hidden.

## Constraint

New constraint: Dashboard must be fully usable at 666px width. No horizontal scrolling. This is the minimum width for side-by-side orchestrator CLI + dashboard workflow on MacBook Pro.

## Options

1. **Wrap to 2 rows** - Stats bar becomes 2-line on narrow screens
2. **Collapse/group** - Combine related stats (e.g., 'ready/blocked' as one item)
3. **Progressive disclosure** - Show fewer items, expand on hover/click
4. **Responsive icons** - Replace labels with icons at narrow widths
5. **Priority ordering** - Most important stats first, others overflow to menu

## Recommendation

Option 1 or 2 - wrapping or grouping maintains visibility of all stats without hiding information.

## Files
- web/src/routes/+page.svelte - stats bar layout
close_reason: Stats bar now uses flex-wrap with grouped items (agent counts, secondary indicators) that wrap gracefully at 666px width instead of horizontal scrolling. Tests pass.

title: Epic: Fix completion lifecycle state synchronization
description:
## Goal

Fix the state synchronization gaps in the agent completion lifecycle that cause:
- 49 zombie in_progress issues (no active agent working on them)
- triage:ready labels persisting after spawn
- NEEDS_REVIEW completions showing for already-closed issues
- 712 accumulated workspaces

## Scope

- Remove triage:ready label when issue transitions to in_progress
- Add reconciliation command to detect and fix orphaned in_progress issues
- Filter closed issues from orch review
- Add workspace cleanup for stale workspaces

## Out of Scope

- Real-time state machine (event-driven sync)
- Automatic cleanup without user confirmation
- Daemon-side reconciliation

## Success Criteria

- [ ] After spawn, issue has in_progress status and no triage:ready label
- [ ] orch reconcile identifies zombie issues and offers to fix
- [ ] orch review doesn't show NEEDS_REVIEW for closed issues
- [ ] orch clean --workspaces removes old workspace directories

## Related

- Investigation: .kb/investigations/2025-12-29-inv-completion-lifecycle-broken-49-zombie.md
- Prior design: .kb/investigations/2025-12-25-design-orchestrator-completion-lifecycle-two.md
close_reason: All 4 children complete: label removal, reconcile command, review filter, workspace cleanup

title: Remove triage:ready label after spawn transitions issue to in_progress
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-9hld: Fix completion lifecycle state synchronization

## Task

After updating beads issue status to in_progress (cmd/orch/main.go:1315), remove the triage:ready label if present.

## Implementation

In cmd/orch/main.go around line 1318, after the UpdateIssueStatus call, add:

```go
// Remove triage:ready label now that issue is being worked on
if err := beads.FallbackRemoveLabel(beadsID, "triage:ready"); err != nil {
    // Don't block spawn on label removal failure - just warn
    fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to remove triage:ready label: %v\n", err)
}
```

May need to add FallbackRemoveLabel helper in pkg/beads/cli_client.go similar to other Fallback* functions.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] After spawn, issue no longer has triage:ready label
- [ ] Spawn doesn't fail if label removal fails
- [ ] Test with issue that has triage:ready label
- [ ] Test with issue that doesn't have triage:ready label
close_reason: Added FallbackRemoveLabel to beads/client.go, RemoveTriageReadyLabel to verify/check.go, and call in spawn command after UpdateIssueStatus. Prevents triage:ready label from persisting after spawn.

title: Add orch reconcile command to detect and fix zombie in_progress issues
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-9hld: Fix completion lifecycle state synchronization

## Task

Add a new command `orch reconcile` that:
1. Lists all in_progress issues from beads
2. Gets all active OpenCode sessions
3. Identifies issues that have no matching active agent (zombies)
4. Offers to either:
   - Reset issue status to 'open' (for re-spawn)
   - Close issue with reason 'orphaned - no active agent'

## Implementation

Create cmd/orch/reconcile.go with:
- `orch reconcile` - Preview mode, shows zombies without action
- `orch reconcile --fix` - Interactive mode, prompts for each zombie
- `orch reconcile --reset-all` - Batch reset all zombies to open
- `orch reconcile --close-all` - Batch close all zombies

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `orch reconcile` shows list of zombie issues
- [ ] `orch reconcile --fix` prompts for action on each
- [ ] Status updates work correctly
- [ ] Test with current 44 zombies
close_reason: Added orch reconcile command (cmd/orch/reconcile.go) that detects zombie in_progress issues with no active agent and offers interactive fix options (reset to open or close). Includes tests, JSON output, project/age filtering.

title: Filter closed issues from orch review NEEDS_REVIEW output
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-9hld: Fix completion lifecycle state synchronization

## Task

Modify `orch review` to check if the beads issue is already closed before showing NEEDS_REVIEW status.

Currently, review.go processes workspaces and shows verification results without checking if the underlying beads issue has been closed. This causes stale NEEDS_REVIEW entries for work that's already complete.

## Implementation

In cmd/orch/review.go, before displaying a completion item:
1. Check if beads issue exists and its status
2. If status is 'closed', either:
   - Mark as OK (closed overrides NEEDS_REVIEW)
   - Add a 'CLOSED' status indicator
   - Filter out entirely from the default view

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Closed issues don't show as NEEDS_REVIEW
- [ ] Clear indication when issue state differs from workspace state
- [ ] `--all` flag still shows everything for debugging
- [ ] Test with orch-go-ptvu (closed but shows NEEDS_REVIEW)

title: Add workspace cleanup option to orch clean command
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-9hld: Fix completion lifecycle state synchronization

## Task

Add `--workspaces` flag to `orch clean` that removes workspace directories for completed/closed issues.

Currently 712 workspaces have accumulated in .orch/workspace/, causing:
- Slow orch status (reads all workspaces)
- Disk space consumption
- Stale verification data

## Implementation

Add to cmd/orch/clean.go:
- `orch clean --workspaces` - Remove workspaces for closed issues
- `orch clean --workspaces --older-than 7d` - Remove workspaces older than N days
- `orch clean --workspaces --dry-run` - Show what would be removed

Logic:
1. List all workspace directories
2. For each, check if linked beads issue is closed
3. If closed (or older than threshold), mark for removal
4. Prompt for confirmation unless --force

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `orch clean --workspaces --dry-run` shows cleanable workspaces
- [ ] Workspaces for closed issues are removed
- [ ] Workspaces for open/in_progress issues are preserved
- [ ] --older-than flag works with day specification
- [ ] Confirmation prompt before removal
close_reason: Added workspace cleanup to orch clean: --workspaces removes old workspace dirs (default >7 days), --older-than N configures threshold, --force skips confirmation, skips workspaces with active sessions. Tests passing, committed.

title: Pre-spawn token estimation to prevent context overflow
description:
## Problem

Spawn can fail with 'prompt is too long: 207879 tokens > 200000 maximum' after creating workspace and session. No warning before spawn.

## Evidence

orch-go-iq2h failed with 207k tokens. SPAWN_CONTEXT.md was only 534 lines (~19KB). The overflow came from combined context (system prompt + CLAUDE.md + skill + spawn context + kb context).

## Solution

Before spawning, estimate total tokens:
1. Count SPAWN_CONTEXT.md tokens (rough: chars/4)
2. Add skill size estimate
3. Add CLAUDE.md sizes (global + project)
4. Add OpenCode system prompt overhead (~10-20k tokens)
5. Warn or abort if approaching 200k limit

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Pre-spawn token estimation
- [ ] Warning at 150k+ estimated tokens
- [ ] Abort at 180k+ with explanation
- [ ] Suggest mitigations (skip kb context, use smaller skill)
close_reason: Implemented pre-spawn token estimation with warning at 100k tokens and error at 150k tokens. See pkg/spawn/tokens.go

title: Agents should report BLOCKED status instead of going idle silently
description:
## Problem

When agents hit technical walls (e.g., need browser tools but don't have them), they go idle silently. No beads comment indicating blocked status. Orchestrator has to infer from lack of progress.

## Evidence

From batch spawn failure analysis (pw-oq32.3):
- Agent reached 'visual verification' step
- No browser tools available
- Agent went idle - no further comments
- Orchestrator didn't know agent was stuck vs still working

## Solution

Add BLOCKED comment pattern for stuck agents.

**Skill guidance update:**
- Add to worker skills: 'If blocked on external dependency, report via beads comment'
- Pattern: `bd comment <id> 'BLOCKED: <reason>'`
- Examples:
  - 'BLOCKED: Cannot verify UI - no browser tools available'
  - 'BLOCKED: Test requires database connection - not configured'
  - 'BLOCKED: Waiting for human decision on API design'

**orch status enhancement:**
- Parse beads comments for 'BLOCKED:' prefix
- Show blocked agents with different indicator: '⚠️ BLOCKED' vs 'idle'
- Include block reason in status output

**Optional: bd block command**
- `bd block <id> 'reason'` - sets blocked status with reason
- Cleaner than comment pattern, enables filtering

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Worker skill documentation updated with BLOCKED pattern
- [ ] orch status detects BLOCKED comments and shows indicator
- [ ] Block reason displayed in status output
- [ ] Optional: bd block command for explicit blocking

## References

- Source: .kb/investigations/2025-12-29-inv-batch-spawn-failure-analysis.md
close_reason: Implemented BLOCKED status detection in orch status. Agents can now report 'BLOCKED: reason' via bd comment and orchestrator sees '🚫 blocked' indicator. Tests pass, skill documentation updated in orch-knowledge repo.

title: Epic: Unified Orchestrator Session Model
description:
## Goal

Unify orchestrator session management with worker session patterns, giving orchestrators explicit session boundaries, tracking, resume capability, and reflection.

## Context

Investigation .kb/investigations/2025-12-29-inv-unified-session-model-design.md found:
- Workers have complete lifecycle (spawn → track → resume → reflect)
- Orchestrators have fragile ad-hoc flow (76 'where were we?' requests)
- session-transition skill never used (wrong abstraction)
- 'Focus block' is the orchestrator session unit

## Scope

**In scope:**
- `orch session start/status/end` commands (MVP)
- Session state persistence (~/.orch/session.json)
- Integration with existing focus/handoff commands
- Retire session-transition skill

**Out of scope:**
- Beads tracking for orchestrator sessions (use focus tracking instead)
- Automatic session detection (explicit boundaries preferred)
- Full session history (Phase 2)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Orchestrator sessions have explicit start/end boundaries
- [ ] Session status shows duration, spawns, and focus
- [ ] Session end produces handoff document automatically
- [ ] session-transition skill retired
- [ ] 'Where were we?' friction reduced (qualitative)
close_reason: All 5 children complete: orch session start/status/end commands implemented, spawn tracking added, session-transition skill retired

title: Implement orch session start command
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-amfa: Epic: Unified Orchestrator Session Model

## Task

Implement `orch session start ["goal"]` command that:
1. Sets focus (via existing focus.New/Set)
2. Records session start time to ~/.orch/session.json
3. Creates session ID (for tracking spawns)
4. Outputs confirmation with session ID and goal

## Design Notes

Session state file (~/.orch/session.json):
```json
{
  "id": "sess_20251229_103000",
  "started": "2025-12-29T10:30:00Z",
  "goal": "Ship snap MVP",
  "focus_id": "focus_xyz",
  "spawns": []
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `orch session start "goal"` creates session state file
- [ ] Session integrates with existing `orch focus` (reuse focus store)
- [ ] Session ID generated (for spawn tracking)
- [ ] Command outputs session ID and start time
- [ ] Existing `orch focus` continues to work independently
close_reason: Implemented orch session start command (commit 2424381b)

title: Implement orch session status command
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-amfa: Epic: Unified Orchestrator Session Model

## Task

Implement `orch session status` command that shows:
1. Session ID and start time
2. Duration (how long session has been running)
3. Current focus/goal
4. Spawns in this session (count + beads IDs)
5. Active agents (from OpenCode/tmux)

## Design Notes

Output format:
```
Session: sess_20251229_103000
Started: 2025-12-29 10:30 (2h 15m ago)
Goal: Ship snap MVP

Spawns this session: 5
  - orch-go-xyz (complete)
  - orch-go-abc (in_progress)
  - ...

Active agents: 2
  Run `orch status` for details.
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Shows session duration and goal
- [ ] Lists spawns created during this session
- [ ] Integrates with existing `orch status` for agent details
- [ ] Handles no-active-session gracefully ("No active session")
close_reason: Implemented orch session status with compact output format, spawns with status (complete/in_progress), active agents count from OpenCode API, and reference to orch status

title: Implement orch session end command
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-amfa: Epic: Unified Orchestrator Session Model

## Task

Implement `orch session end` command that:
1. Runs `orch handoff` to generate SESSION_HANDOFF.md
2. Prompts for D.E.K.N. Knowledge and Next sections (required)
3. Saves handoff to session workspace
4. Clears session state file
5. Optionally offers cleanup (tmux sessions, stale workspaces)

## Design Notes

Workflow:
```
$ orch session end
Session sess_20251229_103000 ending...
Duration: 4h 30m
Spawns: 8 (6 complete, 2 in_progress)

⚠️  2 agents still running. Continue? (y/n)

Generating handoff...
[D.E.K.N. prompts for Knowledge and Next]

Handoff saved to: ~/.orch/session/2025-12-29/SESSION_HANDOFF.md
Session ended.
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Generates handoff document with session context
- [ ] Prompts for synthesis sections (Knowledge, Next)
- [ ] Warns about in-progress agents before ending
- [ ] Saves handoff to session directory
- [ ] Clears session state after successful end
close_reason: Implemented orch session end with handoff integration: warns about in-progress agents, prompts for D.E.K.N. Knowledge/Next sections, saves handoff to ~/.orch/session/{date}/SESSION_HANDOFF.md, adds --no-handoff and --force flags

title: Track spawns in session state
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-amfa: Epic: Unified Orchestrator Session Model

## Task

Modify `orch spawn` to record spawns in session state:
1. Check if active session exists
2. Append spawn info (beads ID, workspace, timestamp) to session.json
3. Make recording optional (spawns work without active session)

## Design Notes

Session state update:
```json
{
  "id": "sess_20251229_103000",
  "spawns": [
    {"beads_id": "orch-go-xyz", "workspace": "og-inv-foo", "spawned": "..."},
    {"beads_id": "orch-go-abc", "workspace": "og-feat-bar", "spawned": "..."}
  ]
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `orch spawn` records spawn in active session (if exists)
- [ ] Spawning works normally without active session
- [ ] Session status shows spawns from this session
- [ ] No breaking changes to existing spawn behavior

title: Dashboard shows 0 active when CLI shows running agents
description: API returns status='idle' for agents that CLI shows as 'running'. Root cause: API uses time-based DetermineStatusFromSession() which marks agents as idle after 10min without update, but CLI uses IsSessionProcessing() which checks actual OpenCode session state. Need to either: (1) have API call IsSessionProcessing for non-completed agents, or (2) have frontend treat 'idle' as still-visible in Active Agents section.
close_reason: Fix verified working. API returns correct status (2 active matching CLI), dashboard displays agents correctly. All verification tests pass: no idle+Complete edge cases, no status mismatches. Fix committed in 3a834ac0.

comments.author: dylanconlin

title: Dashboard shows 'Waiting for activity' for cross-project agents - SSE only streams current project
description:
**Symptom:** Cross-project agent (pw-t0go) appears in dashboard but shows 'Waiting for activity...' with no real-time updates.

**Root cause:** handleEvents in serve.go only sets x-opencode-directory for the current project (sourceDir). Events from other projects aren't streamed.

**Location:** cmd/orch/serve.go:1147-1149

**Fix options:**
1. Open multiple SSE connections (one per discovered project) and multiplex
2. Query OpenCode for a cross-project SSE endpoint (may not exist)
3. Poll /api/agents periodically as fallback for cross-project agents

**Related:** orch-go-h3nh fixed visibility, this is the SSE counterpart
close_reason: Implemented SSE multiplexing in handleEvents to stream events from all discovered project directories. Uses discoverAllProjectDirs() to find all projects with OpenCode sessions, opens concurrent SSE connections via goroutines, and forwards all events to the client. Limits to 10 connections to prevent resource exhaustion. Cross-project agents will now show real-time activity in dashboard.

title: [orch-go] investigation: concurrent test gamma
Epic: Headless Swarm - Batch Execution with Rate-Limit Management
description:
## Goal

Enable concurrent batch spawning of headless agents with intelligent rate-limit management across Claude Max accounts.

## Scope

**In Scope:**
- Batch spawn command (`orch swarm` or daemon enhancement)
- Concurrency control (configurable max active agents)
- Rate-limit detection before hitting limits
- Auto-account switching when capacity exhausted
- Spawn queuing when all accounts at limit
- Aggregate progress visibility

**Out of Scope:**
- Multi-model routing (flash vs opus based on task complexity)
- Distributed architecture across machines
- UI dashboard (CLI-first)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Can spawn N agents concurrently with `--concurrency N` flag
- [ ] System detects approaching rate limits and switches accounts
- [ ] Spawns queue when all accounts exhausted (no errors from rate limits)
- [ ] `orch status` shows aggregate swarm progress
- [ ] No manual account switching required during batch operations
close_reason: All 6 children complete. Headless swarm infrastructure shipped: capacity tracking, worker pool concurrency, orch swarm command, SSE completion tracking.

title: Add usage/capacity tracking to account package
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-bdd: Headless Swarm epic.

Foundation task: Before we can manage rate limits, we need to know current usage per account.

## Task

Extend `pkg/account/` to track and expose usage capacity:

1. Add `GetUsage(accountName string) (*UsageInfo, error)` function
2. Cache usage data with TTL (avoid hammering API)
3. Expose capacity percentage and reset time
4. Detect 'approaching limit' threshold (e.g., 80%)

## Implementation Notes

- Build on existing `pkg/usage/usage.go` which already fetches from Anthropic API
- Need to make it account-aware (currently uses whatever auth is active)
- Cache in memory with ~1 minute TTL

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Can get usage for any saved account by name
- [ ] Returns capacity percentage (used/limit)
- [ ] Returns time until limit resets
- [ ] Cached to avoid excessive API calls
- [ ] Unit tests for cache behavior

title: Add capacity manager for multi-account coordination
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-bdd: Headless Swarm epic.
Depends on: orch-go-bdd.1 (usage tracking)

## Task

Create new `pkg/capacity/` package to coordinate spawning across accounts:

1. `CapacityManager` struct that tracks all accounts
2. `AcquireSlot() (accountName string, err error)` - get next available account
3. `ReleaseSlot(accountName string)` - mark agent completed
4. `Queue()` behavior when all accounts exhausted
5. Auto-refresh usage when account approaches limit

## Key Design Decisions

- Track 'in-flight' agents per account (not just API usage)
- Conservative threshold: switch account at 80% capacity
- Queue with timeout when all accounts busy
- Emit events for capacity changes (for monitoring)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Can acquire slot from least-used account
- [ ] Queues when all accounts at limit
- [ ] Auto-switches to fresh account when current exhausted
- [ ] Thread-safe for concurrent access
- [ ] Unit tests with mock usage data
close_reason: Implemented in pkg/capacity/manager.go - AcquireSlot, ReleaseSlot, queue behavior, auto-refresh all working

title: Add concurrency control to daemon
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-bdd: Headless Swarm epic.
Depends on: orch-go-bdd.2 (capacity manager)

## Task

Enhance daemon to spawn agents concurrently with controlled parallelism:

1. Add `--concurrency N` flag to `daemon run`
2. Use worker pool pattern (N goroutines)
3. Integrate with CapacityManager for rate-limit awareness
4. Track active agents and wait for slots

## Implementation Notes

- Use `golang.org/x/sync/errgroup` or similar for worker coordination
- Each worker: acquire slot → spawn → wait for completion → release slot
- Respect both concurrency limit AND capacity limits

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `orch daemon run --concurrency 3` spawns up to 3 agents in parallel
- [ ] Respects rate limits via CapacityManager
- [ ] Graceful shutdown (wait for active agents)
- [ ] Progress output shows active/queued/completed counts
- [ ] Integration test with mock spawner
close_reason: Added WorkerPool-based concurrency control with --concurrency flag. Pool provides semaphore-based slot acquisition, integrated with Daemon. Tests passing (75.2% coverage).

title: Add batch spawn command (orch swarm)
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-bdd: Headless Swarm epic.
Depends on: orch-go-bdd.3 (concurrent daemon)

## Task

Add new `orch swarm` command for explicit batch operations:

```bash
# Spawn from list of issues
orch swarm --issues proj-1,proj-2,proj-3 --concurrency 2

# Spawn all ready issues
orch swarm --ready --concurrency 3

# Spawn with specific skill
orch swarm --skill investigation --issues proj-1,proj-2
```

## Implementation Notes

- Builds on concurrent daemon infrastructure
- Focused on explicit batch operations (vs daemon's continuous polling)
- Returns when all spawned agents complete (or --detach for fire-and-forget)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Can spawn list of specific issues concurrently
- [ ] Can spawn all triage:ready issues
- [ ] Shows aggregate progress (spawned/active/completed/failed)
- [ ] Respects rate limits via CapacityManager
- [ ] `--detach` option for fire-and-forget
close_reason: Implemented orch swarm command with --issues, --ready, --concurrency, --detach flags. Progress display shows spawned/active/completed/failed. All tests pass.

title: Enhance status command with swarm progress
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-bdd: Headless Swarm epic.
Can be done in parallel with other tasks.

## Task

Enhance `orch status` to show aggregate swarm information:

```
$ orch status
SWARM STATUS
  Active:    3/5 (concurrency limit)
  Queued:    2
  Completed: 7 (today)
  
ACCOUNTS
  personal:  45% used (resets in 2h)
  work:      12% used (resets in 5h)
  
ACTIVE AGENTS
  SESSION ID     BEADS ID    SKILL           ACCOUNT    RUNTIME
  ses_abc123     proj-1      investigation   personal   5m23s
  ses_def456     proj-2      feature-impl    personal   2m11s
  ses_ghi789     proj-3      debugging       work       45s
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Shows active/queued/completed counts
- [ ] Shows per-account usage percentages
- [ ] Shows which account each agent is using
- [ ] Runtime duration per agent
- [ ] Works with `--json` for scripting

title: Add SSE-based completion tracking for headless agents
description:
## Context

Part of orch-go-bdd: Headless Swarm epic.
Depends on: orch-go-bdd.3 (concurrent daemon needs to know when agents complete)

## Task

Ensure headless agents are properly tracked via SSE events:

1. Enhance `CompletionService` to track all headless agents
2. Emit completion events that CapacityManager can consume
3. Update registry when headless agents complete
4. Release capacity slot on completion

## Implementation Notes

- Current `pkg/opencode/service.go` handles SSE monitoring
- Need to integrate with CapacityManager.ReleaseSlot()
- Must handle disconnection/reconnection gracefully

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Headless agent completion triggers slot release
- [ ] Registry updated on completion
- [ ] Handles SSE disconnection gracefully
- [ ] Works with multiple concurrent agents
- [ ] Logs completion events for debugging
