title: Swarm Dashboard Bug Fixes (Audit Dec 2025)
description:
## Problem

Playwright tests for swarm dashboard failing with 6 failures out of 19 tests. Manual audit identified several bugs and inconsistencies that need fixing.

## Evidence

Test run: 13 passed, 6 failed
- `filtering.spec.ts`: 2 failures (status options count, duplicate Clear buttons)
- `race-condition.spec.ts`: 4 failures (hardcoded port instead of using playwright baseURL)

Additional issues found during manual code review:
- Agent grid uses index as key instead of agent.id (causes stale data on reorder)
- Svelte 5 runes inconsistently mixed with Svelte 4 syntax across components

## Scope

Fix all 6 test failures and the keying bug. Svelte 5 consistency is lower priority.

## Files Affected

- `web/src/routes/+page.svelte` - keying bug, Clear button duplication
- `web/tests/filtering.spec.ts` - test needs update for 5 status options
- `web/tests/race-condition.spec.ts` - use baseURL instead of hardcoded port
close_reason: Complete - 6→2 test failures fixed, remaining are real bugs tracked separately

title: Fix status filter test: expects 4 options but UI has 5 (includes 'idle')
description:
## Problem

Playwright test `filtering.spec.ts:11` expects status filter dropdown to have 4 options (All, Active, Completed, Abandoned) but the actual UI has 5 options including 'Idle'.

## Evidence

Test failure:
```
Error: expect(locator).toHaveCount(expected) failed
Locator: getByTestId('status-filter').locator('option')
Expected: 4
Received: 5
```

Source code at `web/src/routes/+page.svelte:253-259`:
```svelte
<select id="status-filter" ...>
  <option value="all">All status</option>
  <option value="active">Active</option>
  <option value="idle">Idle</option>
  <option value="completed">Completed</option>
  <option value="abandoned">Abandoned</option>
</select>
```

## Fix

Update `web/tests/filtering.spec.ts:22` to expect 5 options:
```ts
await expect(options).toHaveCount(5); // All, Active, Idle, Completed, Abandoned
```

title: Fix duplicate 'Clear' button selector ambiguity in tests
description:
## Problem

Playwright test `filtering.spec.ts:85` fails because there are 2 elements matching 'Clear' button when filters are active:
1. The filter bar's Clear button
2. The 'Clear filters' link in the empty state

## Evidence

Test failure:
```
Error: strict mode violation: getByRole('button', { name: 'Clear' }) resolved to 2 elements:
1) <button class="text-xs text-muted-foreground...">Clear</button>
2) <button class="...mt-1 h-auto p-0 text-xs">Clear filters</button>
```

## Fix

Option A: Update test to use more specific selector:
```ts
const clearButton = page.getByTestId('filter-bar').getByRole('button', { name: 'Clear' });
```

Option B: Add data-testid to the Clear button in +page.svelte:
```svelte
<button onclick={clearFilters} class="..." data-testid="clear-filters-button">
```
close_reason: Fixed duplicate Clear button selector. Added data-testid='clear-filters-button' for unambiguous selection. Updated all filtering tests to switch to historical mode. All 8 tests pass.

title: Fix race-condition tests using hardcoded port instead of baseURL
description:
## Problem

All 4 tests in `race-condition.spec.ts` fail because they hardcode `http://localhost:5188` instead of using the playwright config's `baseURL` (`http://localhost:4173`).

## Evidence

Test failures:
```
Error: page.goto: net::ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED at http://localhost:5188/
```

Playwright config at `web/playwright.config.ts:11`:
```ts
use: {
  baseURL: 'http://localhost:4173',
  ...
}
```

But `race-condition.spec.ts:15,43,58,73` all use:
```ts
await page.goto('http://localhost:5188');
```

## Fix

Replace all hardcoded URLs with relative paths:
```ts
await page.goto('/');  // Uses playwright baseURL
```

This matches how other tests (filtering.spec.ts, dark-mode.spec.ts) work.

title: Fix agent grid using index as key instead of agent.id
description:
## Problem

The agent grid in `+page.svelte` uses array index as the Svelte each block key instead of agent.id. This causes stale data issues when agents are reordered (due to sorting) because Svelte reuses DOM elements incorrectly.

## Evidence

Source code at `web/src/routes/+page.svelte:299`:
```svelte
{#each filteredAgents as agent, idx (idx)}
  <AgentCard {agent} />
{/each}
```

Using `idx` as the key is a known anti-pattern. When sorting changes the order, Svelte may bind old agent data to reused DOM nodes.

## Fix

Use a unique identifier as the key:
```svelte
{#each filteredAgents as agent (agent.session_id || agent.id)}
  <AgentCard {agent} />
{/each}
```

Prefer `session_id` (guaranteed unique from OpenCode) with `id` as fallback for legacy/tmux agents.
close_reason: Batch close from orch review

Only event loops (agentlogEvents, sseEvents) use index keys, but these are for non-agent event items.

title: Standardize Svelte 5 runes usage across dashboard components
description:
## Problem

The dashboard mixes Svelte 5 runes syntax with Svelte 4 reactive syntax inconsistently across components:

- `+layout.svelte`: Uses Svelte 5 (`$derived.by`, `$props`)  
- `theme-toggle.svelte`: Uses Svelte 5 (`$derived`)
- `+page.svelte`: Uses Svelte 4 (`$:` reactive statements, `$store` auto-subscription)
- Stores (`agents.ts`, `agentlog.ts`, `theme.ts`): Use Svelte 4 writable/derived

## Evidence

Prior investigation found that mixing runes with Svelte 4 syntax caused the '0 agents displayed' bug:
- See: `.kb/investigations/2025-12-22-debug-dashboard-shows-0-agents-despite-api-returning-209.md`

The current state is safe (each component is internally consistent), but inconsistent across the codebase.

## Recommendation

Low priority - the current state works. Options:
1. Standardize on Svelte 4 syntax (simpler, matches stores)
2. Migrate to Svelte 5 runes (better reactivity, TypeScript)

Decision should be made intentionally, not ad-hoc.
close_reason: Deferred - low priority, dashboard functional

title: Implement skillc verify command
description:
## Problem

Skillc compiles skills with output constraints (outputs.required patterns) but doesn't verify that agents actually produced the required artifacts. The teeth are missing - skills are reminders, not gates.

## Solution

Add `skillc verify` command that:
1. Loads manifest outputs.required patterns
2. Expands interpolation variables ({date} → 2025-12-25, {workspace} → current dir)
3. Globs for matching files
4. Validates structure (D.E.K.N. block exists, self-review checklist complete)
5. Returns exit code 0 (pass) or 1 (fail)

## Usage

```bash
skillc verify [skill-path]
# ✓ Output exists: .kb/investigations/2025-12-25-inv-*.md
# ✓ D.E.K.N. summary present  
# ✗ Self-review incomplete: 'Did you run a test?' not checked
# Exit code 1
```

## Implementation

Build on existing infrastructure:
- manifest.go already parses outputs.required with Pattern and Description
- checker package already validates checksums, budgets, links
- Add pkg/verifier/verifier.go for output and structure verification
- Add `verify` case to main.go command switch

## Structure Validation (skill-specific)

For investigation skill:
- D.E.K.N. block: `## Summary (D.E.K.N.)` section exists
- Self-review: all `- [x]` items checked (not `- [ ]`)

Structure rules could be defined in manifest:
```yaml
outputs:
  required:
    - pattern: '.kb/investigations/{date}-inv-*.md'
      structure:
        required_sections: ['## Summary (D.E.K.N.)', '## Findings']
        checklist_complete: true
```

## Why This Matters

This makes skillc a **discipline engine**, not just a compiler. The skill becomes self-enforcing - you can't call it done without the gate passing.

Grounded in Gate Over Remind principle: reminders fail under cognitive load, gates don't.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `skillc verify` command exists
- [ ] Expands {date} pattern to current date
- [ ] Checks file existence via glob
- [ ] Validates D.E.K.N. section exists (for investigation)
- [ ] Validates self-review checklist complete
- [ ] Returns exit code 1 on failure
- [ ] JSON output mode (--json) for pipeline integration
close_reason: Implemented skillc verify command with pattern interpolation ({date}), glob-based file checks, D.E.K.N. detection, self-review checklist validation, and JSON output. All tests pass. Commit: b138bd0

title: orch learn suggests wrong remediation type for non-constraint gaps
description:
Current behavior: All recurring gaps suggest 'kn constrain' as remediation, even when the gap is just 'topic X had no prior context'.

Problem: 'load test dashboard' recurring 3x suggests 'kn constrain "load test dashboard"' - but that's not a constraint, it's completed work that created its own artifacts.

Gap types that exist:
1. Constraint gaps - 'Never do X' rules → kn constrain (correct)
2. Decision gaps - 'We chose X' → kn decide (not suggested)
3. Done work gaps - Work completed, artifacts exist → no action or 'resolved by work'

Suggested fix: 
- Analyze gap context to suggest appropriate remediation type
- Add 'completed-work' resolution type (currently only: added_knowledge, created_issue, investigated, wont_fix, custom)
- Or: Let user specify remediation type when acting

Reference: Gap tracker data at ~/.orch/gap-tracker.json shows gap_type field (no_decisions, no_constraints, sparse_context) that could inform better suggestions.
close_reason: Fixed orch learn to suggest investigation for no_context gaps instead of incorrectly suggesting kn decide. Now remediation types properly match gap types.

title: Dashboard shows all dead agents forever - need time-based cutoff
description:
Dashboard displays dead agents from days ago (24h, 41h, 68h, 124h shown in screenshot). Current logic intentionally keeps dead agents visible 'so user knows they need attention' but this clutters the view.

## Current Behavior (serve.go:1146)
Dead agents are explicitly excluded from the display threshold filter - they show forever.

## Expected Behavior  
Dead agents should have their own display threshold (e.g., 2-4 hours). Old dead sessions don't need attention - they're historical.

## Fix
Add separate deadDisplayThreshold (e.g., 4 hours) and filter dead agents older than that.

## Related
- orch-go-l7eu: Detect stale/zombie sessions (in progress)
- orch-go-c1a8: orch status should detect dead/orphaned sessions
close_reason: Added 4-hour cutoff for dead agents display

title: orch status shows stale architect recommendations after issue closed
description:
**Symptom:** `orch status` shows '1 architect recommendation(s) awaiting review' for orch-go-ow3g, but:
- `bd show orch-go-ow3g` shows Status: closed
- `orch review --architects` correctly shows 'No architect recommendations awaiting review'

**Root cause:** Likely caching architect workspaces without checking beads issue status.

**Location:** Probably in cmd/orch/status.go or wherever architect recommendations are surfaced.
close_reason: Fixed stale architect recommendations in orch status by adding filterClosedIssues() to getCompletionsForSurfacing(). Root cause: lightweight function dropped filtering with verification, but filtering is data integrity. Tests pass, smoke test verified.

title: GetIssuesBatch uses List(nil) which only returns open issues - closed issues not found
description:
Root cause of stale agents showing in orch status:

GetIssuesBatch in pkg/verify/check.go calls client.List(nil) which defaults to open issues only. Closed issues are not returned, so their status isn't populated, and orch status doesn't filter them out.

Fix: Use ListArgs{IDs: beadsIDs} to fetch specific issues regardless of status, or use individual Show calls.

Evidence:
- bd list returns only open issues by default
- bd list --status closed returns closed issues
- orch status --json shows is_completed: null for all agents
- bd show 51jz correctly shows status: closed

See: pkg/verify/check.go GetIssuesBatch function
close_reason: Superseded by q03k which completed the fix

title: Add FAILURE_REPORT.md template and orch abandon --reason flag
description:
From inv-failure-mode-artifacts investigation:

**Problem:** Successful agents create SYNTHESIS.md, but abandoned agents leave only SPAWN_CONTEXT.md. No structured failure artifacts exist. 33 abandoned events in events.jsonl have no diagnostic value.

**Three failure modes identified:**
- Context exhaustion (~60%) - agent makes progress then stops
- External blocker (~25%) - agent asks question, never gets answer  
- Unresponsive/stuck (~15%) - no progress after spawn

**Solution:**
1. FAILURE_REPORT.md template in workspace on abandon
2. `orch abandon <id> --reason 'context exhausted'` flag
3. Auto-comment on beads: `Phase: Abandoned - [reason]`

**Template:**
```markdown
# Failure Report
**Agent:** {workspace-name}
**Issue:** {beads-id}
**Failure Mode:** {context-exhaustion | blocked | unresponsive | other}
**Last Phase:** {phase from beads}

## What Was Tried
## Failure Reason  
## Recommendations for Retry
```

Ref: .kb/investigations/2025-12-21-inv-failure-mode-artifacts.md

title: Enhance kb-reflect skill to propose issues from findings
description:
**Problem:** Current kb-reflect skill is a procedure manual. Agent runs kb reflect, reports findings, but doesn't act. Triage falls to orchestrator manually reading output.

**Proposed Enhancement:**

Agent applies decision trees from skill and produces actionable proposals in SYNTHESIS.md:
- "Archive 3 stale investigations: X, Y, Z"
- "Create decision for topic with 5 investigations" 
- "Fix drift: constraint X outdated"

Orchestrator reviews proposals, approves/adjusts, then issues get created.

**Why this approach:**
- Gate Over Remind: Turns guidance into action
- Surfacing Over Browsing: Agent does triage work, orchestrator just approves
- Matches agent→orchestrator pattern

**Implementation:**
1. Update kb-reflect skill to include 'Proposed Actions' section in SYNTHESIS.md
2. Each proposal includes: finding type, what to do, why, draft issue title/description
3. Orchestrator can approve (agent creates issues) or adjust manually

**Success criteria:**
- Running kb-reflect produces actionable proposals, not just reports
- Orchestrator time reduced from 'read all findings' to 'approve/reject proposals'

**References:**
- Skill: ~/orch-knowledge/skills/src/worker/kb-reflect/SKILL.md
- Discussion: Two-model session on kb ask design surfaced this gap

title: Implement kb supersede command for decision replacement
description:
From architect design session (orch-go-8sa5). When a new project's decision replaces an old one, both should link to each other.

Command: kb supersede <old-artifact> --by <new-artifact>

Behavior:
- Adds 'superseded-by: <new-path>' to old artifact header
- Adds 'supersedes: <old-path>' to new artifact header
- Two-way linking ensures discovery from either direction

Example: skillc decision supersedes orch-cli skill building decision

See: .kb/investigations/2025-12-22-inv-design-knowledge-system-project-extraction.md

title: Untracked agents cannot report phase - status always shows stalled
description:
## Problem

Untracked agents (spawned with --no-track) always show as 'stalled' in orch status, even when they've completed their work.

## Root Cause

Phase detection in GetPhaseStatus() relies on beads comments (bd comment <id> 'Phase: X'). Untracked agents have no beads issue, so:
1. Agent writes .phase file to workspace (e.g., 'Complete')
2. orch status calls GetPhaseStatus(beadsID) 
3. GetPhaseStatus looks for beads comments → none found
4. No phase detected → status shows 'stalled'

## Evidence

```
# Spawned untracked agent
orch spawn investigation 'test' --no-track

# Agent completed, wrote .phase file
cat .orch/workspace/og-inv-quick-test-verify-02jan/.phase
# Output: Complete

# But status shows stalled
orch status | grep untracked
# Output: orch-go-untracked-1767391073 ⚠️ stalled -  investigation 3m 52s
```

## Proposed Fix

For untracked agents, fall back to reading .phase file from workspace:
1. If beadsID starts with 'untracked-' or has no beads issue
2. Find workspace by session ID or beads ID pattern
3. Read .phase file if it exists
4. Use that as the phase source

Alternatively: untracked agents could write phase to a different location that status checks.
close_reason: Already completed: Fixed untracked agent workspace lookup by adding session ID to workspace cache mapping. Tests: go te

title: Epic: Orchestrator Session Lifecycle Parity
description:
## Goal

Implement gates and artifacts for orchestrator sessions to match worker lifecycle rigor, while respecting orchestrator's composite nature.

## Problem

Workers get full lifecycle machinery (SPAWN_CONTEXT.md, phase tracking, beads comments, required SYNTHESIS.md, orch complete verification gates). Orchestrators have partial parity (orch session start/status/end exists) but lack:

- Verification gates at session end
- Required artifacts (can skip with --no-handoff)
- Friction capture prompts
- ORCH_SESSION_CONTEXT.md template

This violates the 'Gate Over Remind' principle: workers have gates, orchestrators have reminders.

## Scope

### In Scope
- Enhanced `orch session end` prompts (add Friction + System Reaction)
- Verification gate (require handoff OR explicit skip with reason)
- Session workspace formalization (~/.orch/session/{date}/)
- ORCH_SESSION_CONTEXT.md template at session start

### Out of Scope
- Beads tracking for orchestrator sessions (different model)
- Phase tracking for orchestrators (not applicable)
- Changes to worker lifecycle (already complete)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `orch session end` prompts for Friction and System Reaction (in addition to Knowledge and Next)
- [ ] Cannot end session without handoff OR explicit `--skip-reflection --reason "why"`
- [ ] SESSION_HANDOFF.md includes Friction and System Reaction sections
- [ ] At least one friction item leading to system improvement (validates the flow)

## Evidence

Investigation: .kb/investigations/2026-01-01-inv-orchestrator-session-lifecycle-parity-workers.md

Prior investigations:
- 2025-12-29-inv-unified-session-model-design.md (designed session commands)
- 2026-01-01-inv-session-end-reflection-ritual.md (designed friction capture)

Principle: Gate Over Remind (~/.kb/principles.md:146-163)
close_reason: Epic complete: All 4 children closed. Orchestrator sessions now have lifecycle parity with workers.

title: Add Friction and System Reaction prompts to orch session end
description:
## Context

Part of Epic: Orchestrator Session Lifecycle Parity (orch-go-pc5u)

## Task

Enhance `orch session end` to prompt for Friction and System Reaction in addition to the existing Knowledge and Next prompts.

## Current State

`cmd/orch/session.go:366-395` prompts for:
- Knowledge (What was learned this session?)
- Next (Recommended actions for next session?)

## Implementation

Add after the Next prompt:

```go
// Friction prompt
fmt.Println()
fmt.Println("Friction (What was harder than it should have been?):")
fmt.Println("  Tool issues, context gaps, process friction.")
fmt.Print("  > ")
friction, _ := reader.ReadString('\n')
friction = strings.TrimSpace(friction)
if friction == "" {
    fmt.Println("  (skipped)")
}
handoffData.DEKN.Friction = friction

// System Reaction prompt
fmt.Println()
fmt.Println("System Reaction (Does this suggest improvements?):")
fmt.Println("  Skill update, CLAUDE.md update, new tooling?")
fmt.Print("  > ")
reaction, _ := reader.ReadString('\n')
reaction = strings.TrimSpace(reaction)
if reaction == "" {
    fmt.Println("  (skipped)")
}
handoffData.DEKN.SystemReaction = reaction
```

Also update DEKNSummary struct in handoff.go to add:
```go
Friction       string `json:"friction,omitempty"`
SystemReaction string `json:"system_reaction,omitempty"`
```

And update the handoff markdown template to include these new sections.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `orch session end` prompts for Friction after Next
- [ ] `orch session end` prompts for System Reaction after Friction
- [ ] SESSION_HANDOFF.md includes Friction and System Reaction sections
- [ ] Prompts can be skipped (empty input is valid)

## Design Reference

.kb/investigations/2026-01-01-inv-session-end-reflection-ritual.md

title: Add verification gate to orch session end
description:
## Context

Part of Epic: Orchestrator Session Lifecycle Parity (orch-go-pc5u)

## Task

Add a verification gate to `orch session end` that requires either:
1. Handoff saved (all prompts answered or defaulted)
2. Explicit skip with reason: `--skip-reflection --reason "why"`

This implements the 'Gate Over Remind' principle - workers have gates via `orch complete`, orchestrators should too.

## Current State

`orch session end` has:
- `--no-handoff` flag that skips all prompts (no reason required)
- `--force` flag that skips in-progress agent warning

## Implementation

1. Replace `--no-handoff` with `--skip-reflection`:
   ```go
   var sessionEndSkipReflection bool
   var sessionEndSkipReason string

   sessionEndCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&sessionEndSkipReflection, "skip-reflection", false, "Skip reflection prompts (requires --reason)")
   sessionEndCmd.Flags().StringVar(&sessionEndSkipReason, "reason", "", "Reason for skipping reflection (required with --skip-reflection)")
   ```

2. Validate that --reason is provided when --skip-reflection is used:
   ```go
   if sessionEndSkipReflection && sessionEndSkipReason == "" {
       return fmt.Errorf("--skip-reflection requires --reason flag with explanation")
   }
   ```

3. Log skipped reflections to events.jsonl for pattern detection:
   ```go
   if sessionEndSkipReflection {
       event := events.Event{
           Type: "session.orchestrator.reflection_skipped",
           Data: map[string]interface{}{
               "session_id": session.ID,
               "reason": sessionEndSkipReason,
           },
       }
       logger.Log(event)
   }
   ```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `orch session end --no-handoff` is deprecated (still works but warns)
- [ ] `orch session end --skip-reflection` requires `--reason "why"`
- [ ] Error message guides user to provide reason
- [ ] Skipped reflections logged to events.jsonl

## Principle Reference

~/.kb/principles.md:146-163 (Gate Over Remind)

title: Formalize session workspace structure
description:
## Context

Part of Epic: Orchestrator Session Lifecycle Parity (orch-go-pc5u)

## Task

Formalize the session workspace at `~/.orch/session/{date}/` to contain both session context and handoff artifacts.

## Current State

`orch session end` already creates `~/.orch/session/{date}/SESSION_HANDOFF.md` (see `getSessionDirectory()` in session.go:496-499).

Session state is stored at `~/.orch/session.json`.

## Implementation

1. Create SESSION_CONTEXT.md at session start (not just session.json):
   ```go
   // In runSessionStart()
   sessionDir := getSessionDirectory(session.Started)
   if err := os.MkdirAll(sessionDir, 0755); err != nil {
       // handle error
   }
   
   contextPath := filepath.Join(sessionDir, "SESSION_CONTEXT.md")
   contextContent := generateSessionContext(session)
   os.WriteFile(contextPath, []byte(contextContent), 0644)
   ```

2. Define SESSION_CONTEXT.md template:
   ```markdown
   # Orchestrator Session Context
   
   **Session ID:** {session-id}
   **Started:** {timestamp}
   **Goal:** {goal}
   
   ---
   
   ## Focus
   
   {goal or "No explicit goal set"}
   
   ## Constraints
   
   (Fill as constraints emerge during session)
   
   ## Prior Context
   
   {Link to prior SESSION_HANDOFF.md if exists}
   
   ## Active Agents
   
   (Updated during session via orch status)
   ```

3. Update `orch session status` to show session directory path

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `orch session start` creates `~/.orch/session/{date}/SESSION_CONTEXT.md`
- [ ] SESSION_CONTEXT.md contains session ID, goal, constraints section
- [ ] `orch session status` shows session directory path
- [ ] SESSION_HANDOFF.md saved to same directory (already works)

## Note

This creates parity with worker workspaces that have SPAWN_CONTEXT.md. The orchestrator equivalent is discoverable in the session directory.

title: Update orchestrator skill to reference CLI gates
description:
## Context

Part of Epic: Orchestrator Session Lifecycle Parity (orch-go-pc5u)

## Task

Update the orchestrator skill's Session Reflection section to reference the new CLI gates instead of describing manual checkpoints.

## Current State

The orchestrator skill (lines 1121-1152) describes three checkpoints:
1. Friction Audit
2. Gap Capture
3. System Reaction Check

And says: "Gate: Run at least one of: orch learn, kn command, or explicit skip"

But this is a documentation gate, not a CLI gate. The CLI (`orch session end`) doesn't enforce these.

## Implementation

After the other tasks in this epic are complete, update the orchestrator skill Session Reflection section to:

```markdown
## Session Reflection (Before Ending Orchestrator Session)

**When to use:** Before ending a focus block (user says "wrap up", context limit, natural stopping point).

**Flow:**
1. Run `orch session end`
2. Answer prompts: Knowledge, Next, Friction, System Reaction
3. If friction surfaced, decide: beads issue, kn entry, or skill/template update
4. Handoff saved to ~/.orch/session/{date}/SESSION_HANDOFF.md

**Skip option:** `orch session end --skip-reflection --reason "why"` (logged for pattern detection)

**Why this exists:** Workers have gates via `orch complete`. Orchestrators now have gates via `orch session end`. The prompts ensure friction capture happens even under cognitive load.
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Session Reflection section updated to reference CLI gates
- [ ] Old manual checkpoint description replaced
- [ ] Skip option documented with reason requirement
- [ ] Session workspace path documented

## Dependencies

- orch-go-pc5u.1 (enhanced prompts)
- orch-go-pc5u.2 (verification gate)
- orch-go-pc5u.3 (session workspace)

## Note

This task should be done AFTER the CLI changes are complete.

title: Phase 3: Evaluate spawn session_id capture
description:
Evaluate if registry storage is still needed for spawn command. Session_id capture is the critical dependency.

Current behavior:
- Spawn creates OpenCode session
- FindRecentSessionWithRetry captures session_id (500ms-2s window)
- session_id stored in registry for later ops

Options:
1. Keep minimal registry (agent_id→session_id only)
2. Store session_id in workspace (SPAWN_CONTEXT.md or separate file)
3. Derive session_id on every lookup (adds latency)

Daemon concurrency limiting also depends on fast active count lookup.

Risk: High - session_id capture timing is critical, concurrent spawn race conditions

Blocked by: Phase 1 and 2 proving derived lookups work

title: orch sessions search - query OpenCode session history
description:
## Problem

Orchestrator sessions (interactive conversations) contain valuable insights but aren't discoverable. Unlike spawned agents (which have workspaces), orchestrator sessions only exist in OpenCode's session storage.

Example: 2025-12-25 session discovered 'teeth check' diagnostic pattern. To find it later, you'd need to remember the date or hope it was captured as a kn entry.

## Evidence

OpenCode persists sessions at:
```
~/.local/share/opencode/storage/message/{session_id}/{message_id}.json
```

Messages contain role, content, timestamps, model, cost, tokens.

## Solution

Add session search capability:

```bash
orch sessions search "teeth check"     # Full-text search message content
orch sessions search --date 2025-12-25   # Filter by date
orch sessions list --recent              # Recent sessions with summaries
orch sessions show <session-id>          # View session messages
```

## Implementation

1. Walk ~/.local/share/opencode/storage/message/
2. Parse message JSON files
3. Search content field (need to check if content is stored - may only be metadata)
4. Return session IDs + matching snippets

## Open Question

Need to verify OpenCode stores message *content*, not just metadata. The sample showed tokens/cost but not the actual text. May need to check OpenCode's storage format more thoroughly.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Can search session history by keyword
- [ ] Can filter by date range
- [ ] Returns session ID + context snippet
- [ ] Works for both agent and interactive sessions
close_reason: orch sessions command suite (list, search, show) already implemented and committed. All tests pass. Implementation uses hybrid disk+API approach: disk for session listing, API for message content search.

title: Gate respawn on filled FAILURE_REPORT.md - require learning before retry
description:
## Problem

When `orch abandon` runs, it creates FAILURE_REPORT.md with a template, but nothing requires filling it out. When respawning, the learning opportunity is lost.

This violates **Gate Over Remind** principle - we have a reminder (file exists) but not a gate (must be filled).

## Proposed Solution

Gate on respawn: When spawning from an issue that has a FAILURE_REPORT.md in its workspace, require key sections to be non-template before proceeding.

**Key sections to validate:**
- `What was attempted` - not placeholder
- `Details` under Failure Summary - not placeholder  
- `Root cause analysis` - not placeholder
- `What should be different` - not placeholder

**Behavior:**
- `orch spawn ... --issue X` checks for FAILURE_REPORT.md in prior workspace
- If exists and has placeholders: error with message 'Fill out FAILURE_REPORT.md before respawning'
- `--skip-failure-review` flag to bypass (explicit opt-out, documented)

## Why This Matters

Without the gate:
- Same failures repeat (no learning captured)
- Context for next agent is lost
- Pattern of 'abandon and retry without thinking' develops

## Alternatives Considered

1. Gate on `orch abandon` - Too much friction at abandon time, orchestrator may not know root cause yet
2. Soft warning only - Violates Gate Over Remind, will be ignored under cognitive load

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Respawn blocked if FAILURE_REPORT.md has unfilled placeholders
- [ ] Clear error message pointing to file
- [ ] `--skip-failure-review` escape hatch exists
- [ ] Works for both `orch spawn --issue` and `orch work`

title: Design: Orchestrator-worker bidirectional communication
description:
Current orchestrator-worker model is mostly fire-and-forget. Gaps:
1. Session health: Can't detect dead sessions (see orch-go-c1a8)
2. Response visibility: orch send works but no structured way to see response
3. Worker questions: orch question polls, doesn't notify orchestrator
4. Course correction: No way to guide worker mid-flight without full context
5. Progress: bd comment is one-way, no ack/response

Need design session to scope: What interaction patterns should exist between orchestrator and workers? How does this relate to MCP vs CLI question (workers could expose MCP to orchestrator)?

title: Complete Phase 4: Remove agent registry file
description:
## Problem

The agent registry (~/.orch/agent-registry.json) was necessary when tmux was the only way to manage agents. Now that OpenCode provides REST API + SSE, the registry causes more problems than it solves:
- Drift between registry, tmux, OpenCode, beads (four-layer reconciliation)
- Ghost agents from stale entries
- 500+ lines of merge conflict, tombstone, locking logic

## Context

From synthesis: .kb/investigations/2025-12-21-synthesis-registry-evolution-and-orch-identity.md

The Dec 6 investigation (orch-cli) proposed phased migration:
- Phase 1: ✅ Store agent metadata in beads comments
- Phase 2: ✅ Beads-first lookup with registry fallback  
- Phase 3: ✅ Registry stripped to minimal tmux mapping
- Phase 4: ❌ Remove registry file entirely

## Solution

1. Update `orch status` to query OpenCode API + tmux directly (no registry read)
2. Update `orch complete` to work without registry lookup
3. Remove registry file creation from spawn flow
4. Delete registry reconciliation code
5. Remove `~/.orch/agent-registry.json` management entirely

## Trade-offs

- `orch status` will be ~100-300ms slower (acceptable for interactive command)
- Fulfills Dec 1 architectural vision: 'orch orchestrates, but doesn't own state'

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `orch status` works without reading registry file
- [ ] `orch complete` works without registry lookup
- [ ] No registry file created on spawn
- [ ] Registry-related code removed from pkg/
- [ ] No ghost agent issues

title: GetIssuesBatch needs to resolve short IDs to full IDs before List call
description:
Follow-up from nfrr fix:

The fix used ListArgs{IDs: beadsIDs} but beadsIDs contains short IDs (e.g., '51jz') extracted from OpenCode session titles. Beads List expects full IDs (e.g., 'orch-go-51jz').

Fix options:
1. Resolve short IDs to full IDs using client.ResolveID() before List call
2. Use individual Show() calls which support short ID resolution
3. Update extractBeadsIDFromTitle to include project prefix

Option 2 is simplest since Show already handles short IDs.
close_reason: Fixed short ID resolution - now uses Show() calls which handle ID resolution

title: Implement kb ask command for inline mini-investigations
description:
## Problem

Every investigation spawn does semantic query answering (kb context → read → synthesize), but with 30+ second overhead. Dylan's 'how would the system recommend...' pattern revealed the desire for SYNCHRONOUS/INLINE answers without spawn overhead.

## Proposed Solution

Add `kb ask "question"` command that:
1. Runs kb context with question keywords
2. Reads top N matching artifacts (investigations, decisions, kn entries)
3. Sends to LLM with synthesis prompt
4. Returns answer inline (~5-10 seconds)

## Key Trade-off

| Mode | Latency | Artifact | Use When |
|------|---------|----------|----------|
| `kb ask` | ~5s | None (ephemeral) | Quick questions, exploratory |
| `orch spawn investigation` | ~30s+ | Yes (.kb/investigations/) | Questions worth preserving |

## Implementation Notes

- Optional `--save` flag to create investigation artifact
- LLM integration: use same model as opencode default
- Cost consideration: every ask is an LLM call (unlike kb context which is grep)

## Example Usage

```bash
# Quick inline answer
kb ask "how should we sort the swarm map?"

# Save as investigation if answer is valuable
kb ask "how should we sort the swarm map?" --save
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Returns useful synthesis in <10 seconds
- [ ] Clear guidance on ask vs spawn decision
- [ ] Optional --save for questions worth preserving

## References

- Investigation: .kb/investigations/2025-12-24-inv-investigate-emerging-pattern-how-would.md
- kn decisions: kn-38b772, kn-3e8bd6
close_reason: Implemented orch kb ask command for inline mini-investigations. Runs kb context, reads top N artifacts, synthesizes via OpenCode (~10-20s). Supports --save for investigation artifacts, --global for cross-project search, --limit for artifact count. Tests pass, committed.

title: Implement orch servers command for centralized server management
description:
Add CLI commands to manage dev servers across all projects from a single place.

**Commands to implement (in order):**
1. `orch servers` / `orch servers list` - Show all projects with port allocations and running status (check lsof for actual binding)
2. `orch servers start <project>` - Start servers via tmuxinator (tmuxinator start workers-{project})
3. `orch servers stop <project>` - Stop servers pane (send Ctrl+C to servers window)
4. `orch servers attach <project>` - Switch to servers pane in tmux
5. `orch servers open <project>` - Open localhost:{web_port} in browser
6. `orch servers status` - Summary: X running, Y stopped, Z total

**Existing infrastructure to use:**
- Port registry: ~/.orch/ports.yaml (pkg/port)
- Tmuxinator configs: ~/.tmuxinator/workers-{project}.yml (pkg/tmux)
- Tmux sessions: workers-{project} with 'servers' window

**Constraints:**
- Must detect actual port binding (lsof -i :{port}) not just allocation
- Don't kill agent windows when stopping servers
- Handle missing tmuxinator configs gracefully

**Reference:** .kb/investigations/2025-12-23-inv-explore-options-centralized-server-management.md
close_reason: Implemented orch servers command with 6 subcommands (list, start, stop, attach, open, status) for centralized server management across projects. All tests passing, integration with port registry and tmux verified.

title: Add SESSION_HANDOFF.md template for orchestrator context preservation
description:
From design-minimal-artifact-taxonomy investigation:

**Problem:** Orchestrator sessions end without structured handoff. Next session starts cold, loses context about active agents, pending work, blocking issues.

**Solution:** SESSION_HANDOFF.md template that captures:
- Active agents and their status
- Blocking issues (PRs waiting, bugs, external deps)
- Key decisions made this session
- Recommended next actions

**Template:**
```markdown
# Session Handoff - {date}

## TLDR
[1-2 sentence summary]

## Active Agents
| Agent | Task | Status |
|-------|------|--------|

## Blocking Issues
- [issue]: [why blocked]

## Decisions Made
- [decision]: [rationale]

## Next Session
1. [priority action]
2. [follow-up]
```

**Integration:**
- `orch handoff` command to generate from current state
- Or manual creation in .orch/SESSION_HANDOFF.md

Ref: .kb/investigations/2025-12-21-design-minimal-artifact-taxonomy.md

title: Epic: Pressure Visibility System - Operationalize Pressure Over Compensation
description:
## Goal

Operationalize the Pressure Over Compensation principle by building three layers that make surfacing failures visible and create pressure for system improvement.

## Context

From investigation `.kb/investigations/2025-12-25-inv-pressure-over-compensation-surfacing-mechanisms.md`:
- 8 surfacing mechanisms exist (kb context, hooks, daemon, beads, spawn context, orch status, synthesis, completion)
- 4/8 are passive (require human to invoke)
- Compensation is currently frictionless - human can paste context without friction
- The gap is a 'Pressure Visibility Layer' that detects, surfaces, and learns from gaps

## Scope

**In Scope:**
- Gap Detection: Detect when orchestrator/agent lacks context it should have
- Failure Surfacing: Make gaps visible and painful to ignore
- System Learning Loop: Convert gap observations into mechanism improvements

**Out of Scope:**
- Changing existing surfacing mechanisms (kb context, hooks, etc.)
- Automated context injection (hooks already do this)
- Agent-level introspection (out of scope)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Orchestrator sees gap warnings when context is sparse
- [ ] Compensation patterns are tracked and visible
- [ ] At least 1 gap converts to system improvement per week (beads issue, kn entry, hook)

## Related

- Principle: `~/.kb/principles.md` - Pressure Over Compensation
- Decision: `~/.kb/decisions/2025-12-25-pressure-over-compensation.md`
- Investigation: `.kb/investigations/2025-12-25-inv-pressure-over-compensation-surfacing-mechanisms.md`
close_reason: All 3 children complete - gap detection, failure surfacing, and learning loop implemented

title: Gap Detection Layer - Detect missing context at spawn and completion time
description:
## Context

Part of epic orch-go-re8n (Pressure Visibility System).

The first layer of the Pressure Visibility System: detecting when context is missing.

## Task

Implement gap detection at key moments in the orchestration workflow:

1. **Pre-spawn gap detection:**
   - kb context returns sparse results (<3 matches) → flag
   - Task keywords don't match any knowledge → warning
   - Prior related investigations exist but weren't surfaced → gap detected

2. **Post-completion gap detection:**
   - Agent asked clarifying questions that should have been in spawn context → gap
   - SYNTHESIS.md shows agent 'had to figure out' something documented elsewhere → gap
   - Agent created kn entries that suggest missing context → gap

3. **Session gap detection:**
   - Orchestrator asks for context that kb should have → gap
   - Human pastes context (detected via hook?) → compensation detected

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Sparse kb context warning shows prominently at spawn time
- [ ] Gap detection flags are returned by spawn command
- [ ] At least 3 gap detection heuristics are implemented

## Implementation Notes

See investigation: `.kb/investigations/2025-12-25-inv-pressure-over-compensation-surfacing-mechanisms.md` Finding 5 for three-layer model.

title: Failure Surfacing Layer - Make gaps visible and painful to ignore
description:
## Context

Part of epic orch-go-re8n (Pressure Visibility System).

The second layer: once gaps are detected, surface them prominently.

## Task

Implement visible surfacing for detected gaps:

1. **Spawn-time surfacing:**
   - Show gap warnings prominently (not buried in logs)
   - Consider: block spawn if gap score is too high (gate, not reminder)
   - Show 'you compensated here last session' if pattern detected

2. **Dashboard surfacing:**
   - Add gap visibility to swarm dashboard
   - Show agents that had to compensate
   - Show recurring gap patterns

3. **Session surfacing:**
   - SessionStart hook could show recent gaps
   - 'Pressure report': what the system is missing

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Gap warnings display prominently at spawn time (not just logs)
- [ ] Dashboard shows gap patterns across agents
- [ ] Gaps are harder to ignore than to fix (friction added to compensation path)

## Implementation Notes

Reference Gate Over Remind principle: 'Enforce through gates (cannot proceed without), not reminders (easily ignored).'

Depends on: orch-go-re8n.1 (Gap Detection Layer)

title: System Learning Loop - Convert gap observations into mechanism improvements
description:
## Context

Part of epic orch-go-re8n (Pressure Visibility System).

The third layer: convert observed gaps into system improvements.

## Task

Implement the learning loop that converts gaps into improvements:

1. **Gap → Issue suggestion:**
   - Detected gap suggests beads issue creation
   - 'This gap occurred 3 times - create issue?'
   - Auto-populate issue with gap context

2. **Gap → Knowledge suggestion:**
   - Detected gap suggests kn entry
   - 'Add constraint? Add decision?'
   - Track which gaps become knowledge vs which recur

3. **Pattern analysis:**
   - Which topics frequently lack context?
   - Which skills/spawns have highest gap rates?
   - Recommend hook additions or kb entries

4. **Improvement tracking:**
   - Did the created issue/knowledge reduce gap rate?
   - Feedback loop: improvements that don't reduce gaps → review

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Recurring gaps automatically suggest beads issues
- [ ] Gap → knowledge path is one command
- [ ] System tracks improvement effectiveness

## Implementation Notes

The goal: make the system learn from gaps rather than relying on human compensation.

Depends on: orch-go-re8n.1 (Gap Detection), orch-go-re8n.2 (Failure Surfacing)

title: Untracked agents have no phase visibility - CLI shows stalled, dashboard shows active
description:
Visibility gap for untracked spawns (--no-track):

1. CLI shows 'stalled' with phase '-' because no beads comments exist
2. Dashboard shows 'active' based on OpenCode session status, no stalled warning
3. No phase reporting mechanism without beads

Evidence: Agent orch-go-untracked-1767118797 has 45 messages, CLI shows stalled, dashboard shows active with 'Completed step'.

Fix needed (both):
1. Untracked spawns should report phase somewhere (workspace .phase file? registry metadata?)
2. Dashboard should show stalled warning like CLI does (idle time heuristic)

This breaks orchestrator visibility when using --no-track for ad-hoc work.

title: orch clean --verify-opencode deletes orchestrator's own session
description:
## Problem

`orch clean --verify-opencode` can delete the orchestrator's own active session, causing OpenCode to crash with 'No user message found in stream' error.

The issue occurs in `cleanOrphanedDiskSessions()`:
1. Lists ALL disk sessions for the project directory
2. Gets session IDs tracked via workspace files (`.orch/workspace/*/SESSION_ID`)
3. Deletes any session on disk that isn't in a workspace file

The orchestrator's session is running in the same project directory, is on disk, but ISN'T tracked in a workspace (it's not a spawned agent - it's the main interactive session). So it gets flagged as 'orphaned' and deleted while still running.

After deletion, `SessionPrompt.loop()` continues running but finds no messages → crashes at `packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts:273`.

## Evidence

- `cmd/orch/main.go:3064-3069` - Logic that marks sessions as orphaned if not in workspace files
- `cmd/orch/main.go:3093` - `client.DeleteSession(session.ID)` deletes the orphan
- `packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts:273` - Crash site: `if (!lastUser) throw new Error('No user message found in stream...')`
- Prior knowledge constraint: 'OpenCode x-opencode-directory header returns ALL disk sessions'

Reproduction:
1. Start OpenCode in orch-go directory
2. Run `orch clean --verify-opencode`
3. Observe: Orchestrator's session is listed as orphaned and deleted
4. Crash: 'No user message found in stream'

## Context

This is a race condition during cleanup. The orchestrator is essentially deleting its own session out from under itself. The fix needs to skip the currently active session when cleaning orphans.

Options for fixing:
1. Skip any session that is currently 'busy' (has active loop running)
2. Pass orchestrator's current session ID to clean and exclude it
3. Check if session was updated within last N seconds (recently active)
4. Query OpenCode for sessions with active status

Recommendation: Option 1 or 4 - use OpenCode's session status to skip busy sessions.

title: Daemon capacity count goes stale - needs reconciliation on each poll
description:
Daemon maintains in-memory count of active agents. When agents complete or are abandoned externally (via bd close, orch clean), the count becomes stale. Daemon thinks it's at capacity (3/3) when actual count is lower.

Had to restart daemon twice this session to unblock.

Fix: Reconcile active count against actual OpenCode sessions on each poll cycle, not just trust in-memory state.
close_reason: Fixed daemon capacity stuck by filtering OpenCode sessions to only those updated within 30 min. Root cause: DefaultActiveCount() was returning all 26 sessions including stale ones from days ago, so Reconcile() never freed slots.

title: Agent stall visibility - distinguish stuck vs waiting vs rate-limited vs confused
description:
**Problem:** No visibility into WHY an agent stopped making progress. Today's example: agent had fix ready, listed next steps, then stopped. Showed as 'running/Implementing' for 5+ minutes until we manually tailed it and sent 'continue'.

**Current state:**
- We see: phase, tokens, runtime, status (running/idle/stalled)
- We don't see: last action, stall reason, waiting-for-input state

**What we need:**
1. **'Waiting for input' state** - Agent idle but not stuck, just waiting for continuation
2. **Last activity content** - Not just 'Implementing' but 'listed 6 next steps, stopped'
3. **Stall reason detection** - Rate limited? Waiting? Confused? Hit error? All show same 'running' status now
4. **Proactive alert** - 'Agent idle 2min with pending work' should surface automatically

**Evidence:** Agent orch-go-zdja had 103 messages, fix ready, next steps listed, but appeared stuck. Only discovered via manual 'orch tail'. Sent 'continue' and it resumed immediately.

**Connects to:** orch-go-jb0w (Root Cause Thinking epic) - we built symptom visibility (phase/tokens) but not behavior visibility (what is agent doing, why did it stop)

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Closing: Root cause found in orch-go-cqa5 - agents stop because Claude's conversational pattern treats step-lists as proposals awaiting confirmation. This was addressed with 'no silent waiting' instruction in SPAWN_CONTEXT (orch-go-ss7o). Stall visibility was treating symptom, not cause.

title: orch spawn should auto-start OpenCode if not running
description:
## Problem

After computer restart, `orch spawn` fails with 'connection refused' because OpenCode server isn't running. User must manually start OpenCode first.

## Solution

When `orch spawn` detects OpenCode isn't reachable (connection refused on port 4096):

1. Start OpenCode server in background: `opencode serve --port 4096 &`
2. Wait for it to be ready (poll /session endpoint)
3. Proceed with spawn

## Alternative considered

- launchd/plist for auto-start on login - more system-level, less portable
- Require manual start - current behavior, friction after restarts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] orch spawn auto-starts OpenCode if connection refused
- [ ] Waits for OpenCode to be ready before proceeding
- [ ] Works on both macOS and Linux
- [ ] Logs that it started OpenCode (user knows what happened)
close_reason: Fixed - orch spawn now auto-starts OpenCode if not running, waits up to 10s for it to be ready

title: Dashboard shows stale agent data - name null, Phase Complete but status active
description:
Symptoms observed:
1. `orch status` shows 0 active agents but 5 sessions are running
2. Dashboard shows 'Starting up...' for agent running 7+ minutes
3. API returns `name: null` for active agents
4. Agents with Phase: Complete still marked status: active

API evidence:
```
curl http://127.0.0.1:3348/api/agents | jq '[.[] | select(.status == "active")]'
# Returns 5 agents with name: null, 4 have phase: Complete but status: active
```

This breaks visibility into daemon-spawned work. Likely regression in serve.go agent detection or OpenCode session querying.

title: Add 'no silent waiting' instruction to SPAWN_CONTEXT template
description:
**From investigation:** 2025-12-30-inv-agents-stop-mid-execution-pending.md

**Root cause:** Claude's conversational pattern treats step-lists as 'proposals awaiting confirmation', not skill guidance. Agents list next steps then stop, waiting for approval that never comes.

**Evidence:** Agent orch-go-zdja had fix ready, listed 6 steps, stopped for 20 minutes until orchestrator sent 'continue'. No skill guidance says 'wait' - this is model behavior.

**Fix:** Add to SPAWN_CONTEXT template (pkg/spawn/context.go):

'Listing steps is NOT a stopping point. If you write 'Next steps:' or a numbered action list, execute them immediately. Do not wait for confirmation. Silent waiting is a bug - you are either working, explicitly blocked (Status: BLOCKED), or done (Phase: Complete).'

**Placement:** AUTHORITY or STATUS UPDATES section

**Consistency:** Matches existing AGENTS.md pattern: 'NEVER say ready to push when you are - YOU must push'
close_reason: Added EXECUTION BEHAVIOR section to SPAWN_CONTEXT template in pkg/spawn/context.go. Instruction tells agents that listing steps is NOT a stopping point and they should execute immediately. Tests pass.

title: Detect sessions that spawn but never execute (silent failures)
description:
## Problem

Agent pw-oq32.4 session was created but never executed. ZERO beads comments - agent never ran a single tool call. Failure was completely silent and only discovered during manual post-mortem.

## Evidence

From batch spawn failure analysis:
- Session created: ses_49466ea0effe1f8WB85HOwX8v7
- Workspace exists with SPAWN_CONTEXT.md
- `bd show pw-oq32.4 --json` shows no comments
- No SYNTHESIS.md, no artifacts, no progress
- Complete task failure with no indication

## Solution

Add health check after spawn to detect sessions that fail to start.

**Option A: Spawn-time check**
- After spawn, wait for first beads comment (Phase: Planning) within 30-60s
- If timeout, warn: 'Session may have failed to start - no initial activity'
- Non-blocking warning, doesn't fail spawn

**Option B: Status-time check**
- `orch status` detects sessions with 0 comments after >1 minute
- Shows 'failed-to-start' or '⚠️ NO ACTIVITY' indicator
- `orch doctor` includes this check

**Option C: Both** (recommended)
- Spawn warns if no activity within timeout
- Status surfaces stuck sessions ongoing

## Related Issues

- orch-go-c1a8: orch status should detect dead/orphaned sessions
- orch-go-l7eu: Detect stale/zombie sessions after OpenCode restart

Consider consolidating these into unified 'session health' feature.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] orch spawn warns if no beads comment within 60s of spawn
- [ ] orch status shows indicator for sessions with 0 comments
- [ ] orch doctor checks for failed-to-start sessions

## References

- Source: .kb/investigations/2025-12-29-inv-batch-spawn-failure-analysis.md
close_reason: Implemented failed-to-start detection with 3 mechanisms: (1) 60s post-spawn comment monitoring with stderr warning, (2) '⚠️ stalled' indicator in orch status for agents >1 min with no comments, (3) Session Health check in orch doctor listing stalled sessions. All changes committed, tests passing.

Implementation plan: (1) add hasBeadsComment helper to verify pkg, (2) add post-spawn wait-for-comment in headless spawn with timeout warning, (3) add 'no comments' indicator to AgentInfo and status display, (4) add failed-to-start check in doctor.

title: Redesign 'Needs Attention' blocked issues section - currently misleading
description:
## Problem

The 'DECISION NEEDED' section shows '3 blocked issues' with action '→ bd blocked', but:

1. **Wrong framing** - 'Decision needed' implies human action required, but sequential epic phases being blocked is expected behavior
2. **Useless action** - Clicking 'bd blocked' just shows the same info, doesn't help decide anything
3. **No context** - Doesn't explain WHY it's surfaced or WHAT to do

Current blocked issues are P2 epic phases (6uli.3 blocked by 6uli.2, etc.) - this is correct dependency behavior, not a problem.

## What would be useful

Only surface blocked issues that actually need intervention:

| Scenario | Action Needed | Suggested UI Action |
|----------|---------------|---------------------|
| Blocked by CLOSED issue | Stale dep, unblock it | 'bd dep remove X Y' |
| Blocked by ABANDONED work | Cancel or reprioritize | 'bd close' or reassign |
| Circular dependency | Fix dep graph | Show cycle, offer fix |
| Blocked >7 days | Blocker may be stuck | Surface blocker status |
| Sequential phases | **Nothing** - expected | Don't surface at all |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Don't surface naturally-blocked sequential work (blocked by open earlier phase)
- [ ] Only surface blocked issues where intervention helps
- [ ] Show actionable command, not just 'bd blocked'
- [ ] Explain the problem: 'Blocked by closed issue' not just 'blocked'

title: Dashboard 'Active Agents' section conflates working and dead agents
description:
**Problem:** Dashboard showed '11 Active Agents' when only 3 were truly active. Dead agents (from rate limiting) were mixed in, making the count misleading.

**Evidence this session:** Screenshot showed 11 Active but orch status showed 3 active + 78 dead. Had to mentally filter.

**Root cause:** activeAgents store intentionally includes dead agents 'so users know they need attention' (agents.ts line 241). But this makes Active count misleading.

**Code location:** web/src/lib/stores/agents.ts:240-241
```typescript
export const activeAgents = derived(agents, ($agents) =>
  $agents.filter((a) => a.status === 'active' || a.status === 'idle' || a.status === 'dead' || a.status === 'stalled')
);
```

**Possible solutions:**
1. Split into 'Working (3)' and 'Needs Attention (8)' sections
2. Change label from 'Active Agents' to 'Agent Sessions'
3. Add visual separator between truly active and dead

**Impact:** Orchestrator can't quickly see actual active count.
close_reason: Visual verified: Dashboard now shows Working section separate from Needs Attention. npm run check passes.

title: glass: daemon-based shared browser control
description:
**Status:** Functional - glass binary built and installed at ~/bin/glass

**Implemented:**
- CDP daemon with persistent Chrome connection (pkg/chrome/daemon.go)
- Tab targeting via --tab index or --url pattern
- Focus file (~/.glass-focus) for default tab persistence
- Commands: url, title, tabs, snap, actions, actions-json, focus
- Assert command for orchestrator validation (url-contains, title-contains, selector-exists, text-contains)
- MCP server (glass mcp) with tools: glass_tabs, glass_page_state, glass_elements, glass_click, glass_type, glass_navigate, glass_focus

**Current issue:** MCP tools not appearing in orchestrator session. Investigation spawned (orch-go-ixxg) to determine why playwright_browser_* tools show but glass_* tools don't.

**Prereq:** Chrome must run with --remote-debugging-port=9222 (use launch-chrome.sh)

**Next:**
1. Fix MCP tool visibility issue
2. Dogfood with orchestrator for real browser tasks
3. Decide: disable playwright MCP for orchestrator, keep glass only?

See: ~/Documents/personal/glass/CLAUDE.md for full docs
close_reason: Fixed glass MCP tool double-prefix issue (glass_tabs → tabs in registration, becomes glass_tabs via opencode). Committed fix to glass repo. Investigation recommends disabling playwright MCP (failing, not needed when glass works). kn recorded: MCP tools should not include client name prefix.

title: Headless spawns not discoverable by beads ID after completion
description: When spawning headless (--headless), the session is discoverable while running via OpenCode API. But after completion, there's no way to find it by beads ID because: (1) No tmux window exists to search, (2) Session may be gone from OpenCode in-memory list, (3) Workspace .session_id file exists but tail/question don't search workspaces by beads ID. Potential fixes: (1) Include beads ID in session title for API lookup, (2) Have tail/question search workspace directories by beads ID pattern, (3) Store beads_id→workspace mapping somewhere. Discovered during registry removal validation testing.

title: Improve orch servers init main.go detection heuristics
description:
orch servers init incorrectly detected 'go run ./cmd/gendoc' as an API server for orch-go. The gendoc command is a doc generator, not a server.

Current behavior: Finds go.mod + any main.go → assumes it's a server on port 8080

Better heuristics needed:
- Look for http.ListenAndServe or net.Listen patterns in main.go
- Check if main.go imports net/http or web frameworks (gin, echo, chi, fiber)
- Skip cmd/ subdirectories that are clearly CLI tools (gendoc, migrate, etc.)
- Maybe require explicit port in code or config

Related: pkg/servers/detect.go

title: Fix spawn to use standalone mode with TUI (port Python approach)
description:
## Problem

Workers spawned via orch-go don't show the OpenCode TUI - they run in headless mode with raw text output. This breaks observability and the expected worker experience.

## Root Cause

orch-go uses `opencode run --model X --title Y "prompt"` which passes prompt as CLI argument, triggering headless mode. Python orch-cli uses standalone mode which renders full TUI.

## Current (broken)
```
opencode run --model anthropic/claude-opus --title og-feat-xyz "prompt"
# Runs headless, no TUI
```

## Expected (Python's approach)
```
opencode {project_dir} --model anthropic/claude-opus
# Wait for TUI ready (poll for visual indicators)
# Wait 1.0s for input focus
tmux send-keys -l "prompt"
tmux send-keys Enter
# Full TUI rendered
```

## Implementation

Port from Python orch-cli (spawn.py:837-1127):

1. **WaitForOpenCodeReady()** - Poll tmux pane for TUI indicators:
   - `┃` (thick vertical bar - prompt box)
   - `build` or `agent` (agent selector)
   - `alt+x` or `commands` (command hints)
   - Timeout: 15s, Poll interval: 200ms
   - Extra 1.0s wait after visual ready for input focus

2. **Modify BuildRunCommand()** - Change from:
   ```go
   args := []string{"run", "--model", cfg.Model, "--title", cfg.Title, cfg.Prompt}
   ```
   To standalone mode:
   ```go
   args := []string{cfg.ProjectDir, "--model", cfg.Model}
   ```

3. **Add send-keys prompt injection** - After TUI ready:
   ```go
   tmux.SendKeysLiteral(window, prompt)  // -l flag for special chars
   tmux.SendEnter(window)
   ```

4. **Optional: DiscoverSession()** - Query API to find session by directory + spawn time for later API interactions

## Reference

- Investigation: .kb/investigations/2025-12-20-design-explore-tradeoffs-orch-opencode-integration.md
- Python impl: orch-cli/src/orch/spawn.py:959-1032 (spawn_with_opencode)
- Python impl: orch-cli/src/orch/spawn.py:1083-1127 (_wait_for_opencode_ready)

## Validation

- Spawn agent via `orch spawn investigation "test"`
- Verify TUI renders in tmux window (header, token counter, purple status bar)
- Verify agent processes prompt correctly

title: Epic: Orch Ecosystem Consolidation (6-month)
description:
Consolidate 8 repos to 4 functional units. See .kb/investigations/2025-12-24-inv-full-ecosystem-audit-scope-simplify.md for full analysis.

## Phases

### Phase 1 (Month 1): kb absorbs kn
- Add kb quick subcommand with kn semantics
- Migrate .kn entries to .kb format  
- Deprecate kn binary

### Phase 2 (Month 2-3): orch-go reaches Python parity
- Port remaining Python features (synthesis, transcript, friction, stale, doc-check, lint, logs, history)
- Add deprecation warnings to Python orch-cli
- Update skill references

### Phase 3 (Month 4): beads abstraction
- Create pkg/beads/client.go interface
- Reduce direct bd CLI calls
- Enable mock testing

### Phase 4 (Month 5-6): Final cleanup
- Archive Python orch-cli
- Evaluate agentlog (archive or integrate)
- Consider skillc integration into orch-go

## Success Criteria
- Single knowledge CLI (kb) instead of two
- orch-go as only orchestration CLI
- All skill references point to valid tools
- beads interaction via abstraction layer

## Evidence
- orch-go: 37k LoC, 334 skill refs
- kb+kn: 231 combined skill refs, conceptual overlap
- agentlog: only 14 skill refs (orphaned)
- Python orch-cli: ~80% parity achieved

title: Orch Ecosystem Identity & Consolidation
description:
Strategic questions surfaced from ecosystem audit that need decisions before implementation work proceeds.

## Context
Four investigations completed 2025-12-24:
- inv-what-orch-ecosystem-reflect-what.md (identity/philosophy)
- inv-full-ecosystem-audit-scope-simplify.md (8-repo consolidation)
- inv-follow-up-ecosystem-audit-orch.md (beads dependency)
- inv-addendum-ecosystem-audit-opencode.md (OpenCode as runtime infra)

## Open Questions

### Strategic (require Dylan input)
1. **Sharing Philosophy** - Patterns only? Tools as OSS? Personal only? Selective extraction?
2. **Identity Framing** - Is 'amnesia-resilient AI orchestration framework' the right framing?
3. **Stabilization vs Evolution** - Lock patterns or keep discovering?

### Structural (have recommendations, need confirmation)
4. **kb + kn merger** - 231 combined skill refs, significant overlap. Merge?
5. **agentlog fate** - 14 skill refs, low adoption. Archive or integrate?
6. **beads abstraction** - Proceed with pkg/beads/client.go interface?

## Evidence Base
- 1,195 beads issues, 1,179 workspaces, 265+ investigations
- 6 principles documented in ~/.kb/principles.md
- 8 repos audited with LoC, ownership, usage metrics

## Deliverable
Decision record capturing answers to all 6 questions, enabling consolidation work to proceed.
close_reason: All 6 strategic decisions resolved. Sharing: selective extraction (principles as writing). Stabilization: core stable, edges evolve. kb+kn: merge under kb. agentlog: archive. beads: proceed with abstraction. source-of-truth: hybrid (beads for tracked work, OpenCode for running sessions). Implementation work can now proceed.

Tracing each principle to its originating failure. This may inform:

title: Decide: Sharing Philosophy
description:
**Question:** How should the orch ecosystem be shared (if at all)?

## Options
A) **Patterns, not tools** - Share principles/docs (like 12-factor manifesto), keep tools personal
B) **Tools as OSS** - Open source orch-go, kb-cli, etc. (significant maintenance burden)
C) **Personal only** - Keep everything private, focus on personal productivity
D) **Selective extraction** - Extract reusable patterns into docs (public), keep tools private

## Context
- Current state: Tools are private, orch-knowledge README describes selective sharing
- 390+ artifacts reflecting Dylan's decisions/workflows/preferences
- Principles document is already publishable quality
- Tools are deeply personal (not designed for others)

## Considerations
- Maintenance burden of OSS
- Value of sharing vs cost of support
- What form would sharing take? (blog posts? manifesto? repo?)

## Deliverable
Decision record with chosen option and rationale.
close_reason: Decision: Selective extraction - share principles/patterns as writing (blog posts, manifesto-style docs), keep tools private. Grounded in Session Amnesia (tools optimized for Dylan's workflow) and Reflection Before Action (avoid support burden). Concrete form: blog post series on principles, not a repo.

title: Decide: Identity Framing
description:
**Question:** What is the right identity/framing for the orch ecosystem?

## Options
- 'Amnesia-resilient AI orchestration framework'
- 'AI agent session infrastructure'
- 'LLM-first development environment'
- 'Personal AI orchestration system'
- Other?

## Investigation Finding
This is **infrastructure for AI agent sessions** - equivalent of:
- dotfiles → configure shells → this configures AI sessions
- CI/CD → orchestrates builds → this orchestrates agent work
- observability → tracks services → this tracks agent sessions

## Key Insight
The core constraint is Session Amnesia - every pattern exists to compensate for Claude having no memory between sessions. This isn't a productivity tool or project management system.

## Deliverable
Chosen identity framing for use in docs, README, and communication.
close_reason:
Decided: 'Amnesia-resilient AI orchestration framework'

## Rationale
- Positions the core constraint (session amnesia) as the defining feature
- 'Framework' signals something with structure/patterns others could learn from
- Technical framing that speaks to engineers who understand the problem
- Accurate to what the system actually is

## Implications for other decisions
- Sharing: Favors sharing patterns/philosophy (the 'amnesia-resilient' insight is transferable)
- Stabilization: Framework implies some stability expectation - core patterns should be documented
- Identity should appear in: README, ECOSYSTEM.md, principles preamble

title: Decide: Stabilization vs Evolution
description:
**Question:** Should the system stabilize or keep evolving?

## Options
- **Stabilize** - Lock patterns, document thoroughly, make accessible
- **Keep evolving** - Continue discovering through practice
- **Both** - Stabilize core, evolve edges (define what's core vs edge)

## Context
- orch-go rewrite just completed (active evolution)
- 6 principles now documented (some stabilization achieved)
- Skills actively being refined
- Consolidation work pending (kb+kn, agentlog fate)

## Considerations
- What's 'core' that should be stable? (principles? skill structure? CLI interface?)
- What's 'edge' that can keep evolving? (individual skills? workflow patterns?)
- Does stabilization enable or hinder sharing?

## Deliverable
Decision on stabilization scope and timeline.
close_reason: Decision: Both - stabilize core, evolve edges. Core (stable): 6 principles, five-concern architecture, SPAWN_CONTEXT structure, D.E.K.N. format. Edges (evolve): individual skills, CLI flags/UX, dashboard, integration patterns. Grounded in Evolve by Distinction - can't stabilize what you haven't discovered.

title: Decide: kb + kn Merger
description:
**Question:** Should kb and kn merge into a single knowledge CLI?

## Evidence
- kb: 123 skill references, long-form artifacts (investigations, decisions)
- kn: 108 skill references, quick entries (decide, tried, constrain)
- Combined: 231 references, significant conceptual overlap
- kn entries promote to kb artifacts (explicit promotion path)

## Recommendation from Audit
Merge under kb with subcommands:
- `kb quick decide` (was `kn decide`)
- `kb quick tried` (was `kn tried`)
- `kb quick constrain` (was `kn constrain`)

Or absorb into orch-go as `orch kb` / `orch kn`.

## Trade-offs
- **Merge:** Simpler mental model, one binary, unified search
- **Keep separate:** kn is faster for quick capture, different UX

## Deliverable
Decision on merger approach and timeline.
close_reason: Decision: Merge kn into kb. Structure: kb decide/tried/constrain for quick capture, kb create for long-form. Key constraint: quick commands stay quick (no prompts). Grounded in Surfacing Over Browsing - one tool to surface all knowledge. Removes unnecessary seam while preserving entry velocity.

title: Decide: agentlog Fate
description:
**Question:** Should agentlog be archived, integrated, or revived?

## Evidence
- Only 14 skill references (vs 334 for orch, 307 for bd)
- Last activity: Dec 16 (8 days ago)
- No integration hooks with orch spawn
- Original purpose: 'AI-first CLI for error visibility'
- No evidence of active use in spawn contexts

## Options
A) **Archive** - It was a good idea that didn't get adopted. Move on.
B) **Integrate** - Fold into orch-go as `orch log` subcommand
C) **Revive** - Add spawn hooks, make it automatic, increase adoption

## Considerations
- Is agent error logging still valuable? (yes, probably)
- Why didn't it get adopted? (no automatic integration)
- Is the effort to revive worth it vs other priorities?

## Deliverable
Decision on agentlog's fate with rationale.
close_reason: Decision: Archive agentlog. 14 skill refs vs 334 for orch = empirical non-adoption. Was a reminder, not a gate. Grounded in Gate Over Remind - if error visibility matters, fold into existing gates (spawn context, complete verification), don't revive a failed separate tool.

title: Decide: Beads Abstraction Layer
description:
**Question:** Proceed with pkg/beads/client.go interface abstraction?

## Recommendation from Audit
Yes - create abstraction layer wrapping the 7 bd commands orch-go uses:
- comment, comments, create, list, ready, show, stats

## Evidence
- beads is external dependency (stevey's project, not Dylan's)
- 31MB binary, 347MB repo, complex internal architecture
- 19 exec.Command calls in orch-go using only 7 of 30+ commands
- Interface is already narrow - abstraction is low-cost

## Benefits
- Isolates API changes to single package
- Enables mock testing for daemon/verify
- Reduces coupling to external project

## Trade-offs Accepted
- Still dependent on external project
- No control over beads roadmap

## Deliverable
Confirmation to proceed (or alternative approach).
close_reason: Decision: Proceed with pkg/beads/client.go abstraction layer. Grounded in Graceful Degradation (enables RPC→CLI→sqlite fallback) and Compose Over Monolith (beads is external, abstraction is the swap seam). Already in progress via orch-go-7yrh RPC client work.

title: Policy skills loading for workers despite audience:orchestrator
description:
Workers spawned with feature-impl are getting the orchestrator skill (1224 lines) loaded, causing:
1. Token limit errors (206587 > 200000 max)
2. Wasted context window
3. Confusing guidance for workers

The orchestrator skill has:
- skill-type: policy
- audience: orchestrator  
- spawnable: false

But OpenCode's skill system doesn't respect these fields - it loads all policy skills regardless.

Options:
1. Fix OpenCode to filter by audience field
2. Move orchestrator skill out of policy/ to prevent auto-loading
3. Trim orchestrator skill size (currently 1224 lines)

Discovered when og-feat-real-time-agent-22dec hit token limit on first response.

title: Epic: Cross-Project Change Visibility (orch changelog)
description:
## Goal

Provide Dylan with ongoing visibility into changes across the orchestration ecosystem (skills, CLI commands, kb/kn entries) with semantic context - not just file diffs but meaning (breaking, behavioral, documentation).

## Context

Design session investigation: .kb/investigations/2025-12-29-inv-skill-changelog-cross-project-change.md

Motivated by Claude Code vs OpenCode hooks confusion (orch-go-aqo8) where skill references to wrong platform caused agent failure. Changes were invisible until they caused issues.

## Scope

### In Scope
- `orch changelog` CLI command aggregating changes from ecosystem repos
- Semantic categorization using skill-change-taxonomy (documentation/behavioral/structural × blast radius)
- Dashboard 'Recent Changes' section with feed
- Integration with `orch complete` workflow (like CLI command detection)

### Out of Scope
- Real-time push notifications (defer to future enhancement)
- skillc deploy hooks (can be added as enhancement)
- Automated remediation of stale references

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `orch changelog` shows skill changes from last 7 days with semantic categories
- [ ] Dashboard displays aggregated feed from all ecosystem repos  
- [ ] Changes like Claude Code → OpenCode would be surfaced before causing failures
- [ ] Cross-project visibility works (orch-knowledge changes visible from orch-go)
close_reason: All children complete. orch changelog CLI, API endpoint, dashboard section, orch complete integration, and documentation all shipped.

title: Implement core orch changelog command with git aggregation
description:
## Context

Part of Epic: orch-go-v7qs (Cross-Project Change Visibility)

## Task

Implement `orch changelog [--days 7] [--project orch-go|orch-knowledge|all]` command that:
1. Reads ecosystem repo list from pkg/spawn/kbcontext.go (OrchEcosystemRepos)
2. Runs git log on each repo for specified time range
3. Categorizes by source (skills/, .kb/, cmd/)
4. Outputs structured changelog

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Command parses --days and --project flags
- [ ] Aggregates commits from all ecosystem repos
- [ ] Outputs grouped by date/category
- [ ] Handles missing repos gracefully (warn, continue)
- [ ] Tests cover basic aggregation
close_reason: Implemented orch changelog command with git aggregation across ecosystem repos. Supports --days and --project flags, categorizes commits by source (skills/, .kb/, cmd/, etc.), handles missing repos gracefully. Tests passing.

title: Add semantic parsing layer to changelog (taxonomy categories)

Reference: .kb/investigations/2025-12-27-inv-skill-change-taxonomy.md

## Task

Add semantic parsing to classify changelog entries:
1. Parse conventional commit prefixes (feat:/fix:/docs:/BREAKING:)
2. Infer category from file paths (skills/ → skill-*, .kb/decisions/ → decision-*)
3. Detect blast radius (single file → local, multiple skills → cross-skill)
4. Output human-readable category badges

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Commits categorized by type (documentation/behavioral/structural)
- [ ] Blast radius detected (local/cross-skill/infrastructure)
- [ ] BREAKING changes prominently flagged
- [ ] Fallback parsing when conventional commits not used
- [ ] Tests cover category assignment

title: Dashboard: Replace 'Pending Reviews' with 'Recent Knowledge'
description:
Replace the noisy 'Pending Reviews (72)' section with a 'Recent Knowledge' view.

**What to show:**
- Recent investigations (all, sorted by date)
- Decisions (high signal, rare)
- TLDR/summary line for each

**Key feature: Upvote/Downvote**
- Upvote = 'useful for my mental model'
- Downvote = 'noise, don't surface things like this'
- Track votes for future filtering criteria
- Creates pressure signal: 90% downvotes → agents creating low-value artifacts

**Kill:**
- The 72-item 'Pending Reviews' queue (wrong abstraction)

**Future evolution:**
- Auto-filter based on vote patterns
- Surface similar-to-upvoted, hide similar-to-downvoted
- Stats on what investigation types are useful

Design discussion: orchestrator session 2025-12-28

title: Proactive surfacing of unreviewed architect recommendations
description:
Architect agents produce valuable recommendations (de-bloat skill, beads RPC, UI validation gates) but they accumulate silently in pending reviews without creating pressure.

**Problem:** 23 agents with unreviewed recommendations sitting idle. High-value design work (e.g., 71% skill size reduction) not acted on because nothing prompts review.

**Solution:** SessionStart surfacing in orch-go:
```
⚠️ 7 architect recommendations awaiting review:
  - de-bloat-feature (8 items): Feature-impl skill 71% reduction
  - beads-integration (4 items): Go RPC for machine-speed
  - ui-validation (2 items): 3-tier validation gates
Run 'orch review --architects' to process
```

**Related:** orch-go-z8pd (cross-project gap surfacing) - same pattern, could share SessionStart hook infrastructure.

**Success criteria:**
- Starting orchestrator session surfaces pending architect recommendations
- High-value design work creates pressure to review, not silent accumulation

title: Dead sessions accumulate indefinitely instead of transitioning to final states
description:
## Problem

Dead sessions (💀) accumulate in the registry and UI indefinitely, creating noise and alert fatigue. Currently showing 80+ dead sessions in `orch status`.

## Root Cause Analysis

'Dead' conflates three distinct states:
1. **Agent completed, process exited normally** - 12 agents at Phase: Complete. These finished their work! Not 'dead' in any meaningful sense.
2. **Agent crashed mid-work** - Actually needs attention, should be surfaced prominently
3. **Old garbage** - Sessions from days/weeks ago that were never cleaned up

## Current Behavior (Buggy)

- Dead sessions persist in registry forever
- `orch complete` closes beads issue but doesn't remove from active tracking
- 'Needs Attention' section shows 80+ agents - alert fatigue, nobody attends to them
- No distinction between 'dead and done' vs 'dead and failed'
- No automatic cleanup or state transition

## Expected Behavior

- `orch complete` should transition agent to 'completed' status and remove from active view
- Dead sessions at Phase: Complete should auto-transition to 'completed' (not 'needs attention')
- Dead sessions NOT at Phase: Complete should:
  - Within 1 hour: Surface as 'needs attention' (might be recoverable)
  - After 24 hours: Auto-transition to 'failed' or 'abandoned' with reason
- Registry should not accumulate garbage indefinitely

## Proposed Fix

1. Add state machine for agent lifecycle: active → {completed, failed, abandoned}
2. `orch complete` transitions to 'completed' (not just closes beads)
3. Background cleanup job (daemon or cron) to:
   - Auto-complete dead agents at Phase: Complete
   - Auto-abandon dead agents after 24h with no phase
4. 'Needs Attention' only shows agents dead < 1 hour without Phase: Complete

## Evidence

```
Dead at Phase: Complete: 12 (should be 'completed', not 'dead')
Dead without Complete: 70 (mixture of old garbage and actual failures)
Total dead: 82+
```
close_reason: Added session cleanup to orch complete and differentiated dead states. Dead agents at Phase:Complete now show as 'done' (ready for completion) rather than 'dead' (needs attention). Tests: go test ./cmd/orch/... - PASS (all tests including new test cases for done status)

3. Mechanism for knowledge promotion (kn -> global).
4. Must be machine-readable and human-clear.
